> ````text
> {"content": "# Odysseus\n\n```\n\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n \u22b9 \u08ea \u02d6 \u0aee( \u02f6\u1d54 \u1d55 \u1d54\u02f6 )\u3063  Odysseus vers. 1.0\n\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\u2500\n```\n\n![Odysseus](docs/odysseus.jpg)\n\nA self-hosted AI workspace -- meant to be the self-hosted version of the UI experience you get from ChatGPT and Claude. But with more jank and fun. Running on your own hardware, with your own data -- local-first, privacy-first, and no trojan.\n\n## Features\n  - **Chat** -- chat with any local model or API; adding them is super simple.<br>\u3000<sub>vLLM \u00b7 llama.cpp \u00b7 Ollama \u00b7 OpenRouter \u00b7 OpenAI</sub>\n  - **Agent** -- hand it tools and let it run the whole task itself.<br>\u3000<sub>built on [opencode](https://github.com/anomalyco/opencode) \u00b7 MCP \u00b7 web \u00b7 files \u00b7 shell \u00b7 skills \u00b7 memory</sub>\n  - **Cookbook** -- Scans your hardware, recommends models, click to download and serve.. easy!<br>\u3000<sub>built on [llmfit](https://github.com/AlexsJones/llmfit) \u00b7 VRAM-aware \u00b7 GGUF / FP8 / AWQ \u00b7 fit scoring \u00b7 vLLM / llama.cpp serving</sub>\n  - **Deep Research** -- multi-step runs that gather, read, and synthesize sources into a nice visual report.<br>\u3000<sub>adapted from [Tongyi DeepResearch](https://github.com/Alibaba-NLP/DeepResearch)</sub>\n  - **Compare** -- a fun tool to compare models side by side. Test completely blind, no bias!<br>\u3000<sub>multi-model \u00b7 blind test \u00b7 synthesis</sub>\n  - **Documents** -- YOU write the text, AI is there to assist, not the opposite.<br>\u3000<sub>multi-tab editor \u00b7 markdown \u00b7 HTML \u00b7 CSV \u00b7 syntax highlighting \u00b7 AI edits \u00b7 suggestions</sub>\n  - **Memory / Skills** -- Persistent memory and skills, your agent evolves over time as it better understands you and your tasks!<br>\u3000<sub>ChromaDB \u00b7 fastembed (ONNX) \u00b7 vector + keyword retrieval \u00b7 import/export</sub>\n  - **Email** -- IMAP/SMTP inbox with AI triage built in: urgency reminders, auto-tag, auto-summary, auto-reply drafts, auto-spam.<br>\u3000<sub>IMAP \u00b7 SMTP \u00b7 per-account routing \u00b7 CalDAV-aware</sub>\n  - **Notes & Tasks** -- Quick notes with reminders, a todo list, and scheduled tasks the agent can act on.<br>\u3000<sub>note pings \u00b7 checklist \u00b7 cron-style tasks \u00b7 ntfy / browser / email channels</sub>\n  - **Calendar** -- Local-first calendar with CalDAV sync to Radicale / Nextcloud / Apple / Fastmail.<br>\u3000<sub>CalDAV pull \u00b7 .ics import/export \u00b7 per-calendar colors \u00b7 agent-aware</sub>\n  - **Works on mobile** -- looks and runs great on your phone, not just desktop.<br>\u3000<sub>responsive \u00b7 installable (PWA) \u00b7 touch gestures</sub>\n  - **Extras** -- more to explore, happy if you give it a go!<br>\u3000<sub>image editor \u00b7 theme editor \u00b7 file uploads (vision + PDF) \u00b7 web search \u00b7 presets \u00b7 sessions \u00b7 2FA</sub>\n\n## Demo\nA full, hover-to-play tour lives on the landing page (`docs/index.html`).\n\n<details>\n<summary>Screenshots / clips</summary>\n\n### Chat & Agents\n![Chat & Agents](docs/chat.gif)\n### Deep Research\n![Deep Research](docs/research.gif)\n### Compare\n![Compare](docs/compare.gif)\n### Documents\n![Documents](docs/document.gif)\n### Notes & Tasks\n![Notes & Tasks](docs/notes.gif)\n\n</details>\n\n## Quick Start\n\nDefaults work out of the box: clone, run, then configure models/search/email\ninside **Settings**. Only edit `.env` for deployment-level overrides like\n`APP_BIND`, `APP_PORT`, `AUTH_ENABLED`, `DATABASE_URL`, or a pre-seeded admin password.\n\nOn first setup, Odysseus creates an admin account (`admin` unless\n`ODYSSEUS_ADMIN_USER` is set) and prints a temporary password in the terminal.\nFor Docker installs, the same line is in `docker compose logs odysseus`.\nUse that for the first login, then change it in **Settings**.\n\nContributing? See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for setup, testing, and\npull request guidelines.\n\n### Docker (recommended)\n```bash\ngit clone https://github.com/pewdiepie-archdaemon/odysseus.git\ncd odysseus\ncp .env.example .env       # optional, but recommended for explicit defaults\ndocker compose up -d --build\n```\nOpen `http://localhost:7000` when the containers are healthy. Docker Compose\nbinds the web UI to `127.0.0.1` by default. If the port is taken, set\n`APP_PORT=7001` in `.env` and recreate the container. Set `APP_BIND=0.0.0.0`\nonly when you intentionally want LAN/reverse-proxy access.\n\n### Native Linux / macOS\n```bash\ngit clone https://github.com/pewdiepie-archdaemon/odysseus.git\ncd odysseus\npython3 -m venv venv\nsource venv/bin/activate\npip install -r requirements.txt\npython setup.py\npython -m uvicorn app:app --host 127.0.0.1 --port 7000\n```\nRequirements: Python 3.11+. Cookbook also needs `tmux` for background model\ndownloads and serves. The app itself is lightweight; local model serving is the\nheavy part and depends on the model, runtime, GPU, and VRAM, so small hosts can\nconnect to API or remote model servers instead. Use `--host 0.0.0.0` only when you intentionally want LAN/reverse-proxy access.\n\n### Apple Silicon\nDocker on macOS cannot use the Metal GPU. For GPU-accelerated Cookbook on an\nM-series Mac, run Odysseus natively:\n\n```bash\ngit clone https://github.com/pewdiepie-archdaemon/odysseus.git\ncd odysseus\n./start-macos.sh\n```\n\nIt launches at `http://127.0.0.1:7860`. To expose it to your phone over a trusted LAN/VPN such as Tailscale, bind all interfaces:\n\n```bash\nODYSSEUS_HOST=0.0.0.0 ./start-macos.sh\n# then open http://<tailscale-ip>:7860\n```\n\nThe script also reads `.env` at startup, so `APP_BIND=0.0.0.0` and `APP_PORT`\nset there are picked up automatically without a command-line override each run.\n\nKeep `AUTH_ENABLED=true` (the default) before binding outside loopback. Do not\nexpose this port directly to the public internet. To build a clickable app wrapper:\n\n```bash\n./build-macos-app.sh\n```\n\n<details>\n<summary>Cookbook, GPU, Ollama, and troubleshooting notes</summary>\n\n**Docker bundled services.** Compose starts Odysseus, ChromaDB, SearXNG, and\nntfy. Odysseus and the bundled service ports bind to `127.0.0.1` by default, so\nthey are reachable from the host but not exposed to your LAN/public internet\nunless you opt in.\n\n**Cookbook storage in Docker.** Downloads live in `./data/huggingface`\n(`~/.cache/huggingface` in the container). Cookbook-installed Python CLIs and\nserve engines live in `./data/local` (`~/.local` in the container), so they\nsurvive container recreation.\n\n**Remote servers.** In **Cookbook -> Settings -> Servers**, generate the\nOdysseus SSH key and add the public key to the remote server's\n`~/.ssh/authorized_keys`. From the host you can also run:\n\n```bash\nssh-copy-id -i data/ssh/id_ed25519.pub user@server\n```\n\n**Docker GPU overlays.** CPU-only users can skip this section. Cookbook can\nonly detect GPUs that Docker exposes to the container \u2014 if the host runtime or\ndevice passthrough is not configured, Cookbook sees the iGPU, another card, or\nCPU instead of your intended GPU.\n\nFor NVIDIA, `scripts/check-docker-gpu.sh` diagnoses GPU passthrough and can\noptionally install the host runtime or update `.env`.\n\n```bash\n# Read-only diagnostic (default \u2014 installs nothing, never edits .env):\nscripts/check-docker-gpu.sh\n\n# Print OS-specific install commands without running them:\nscripts/check-docker-gpu.sh --print-install-commands\n\n# Install NVIDIA Container Toolkit on Ubuntu/Debian (requires sudo):\nscripts/check-docker-gpu.sh --install-nvidia-toolkit\n\n# Write COMPOSE_FILE to .env (only when GPU passthrough is confirmed working):\nscripts/check-docker-gpu.sh --enable-nvidia-overlay\n\n# Full assisted setup \u2014 install toolkit, then enable overlay if passthrough works:\nscripts/check-docker-gpu.sh --install-nvidia-toolkit --enable-nvidia-overlay\n```\n\nSafety notes:\n- The app never installs host GPU runtime automatically.\n- The app never edits `.env` automatically.\n- `.env` is only modified when `--enable-nvidia-overlay` is explicitly passed,\n  and only after GPU passthrough succeeds. `--yes` skips prompts but does not\n  bypass the passthrough gate.\n- `.env.bak.*` backups created by `--enable-nvidia-overlay` are ignored by\n  Git and the Docker build context.\n\nTo enable manually without the script, add this to `.env`:\n\n```bash\nCOMPOSE_FILE=docker-compose.yml:docker/gpu.nvidia.yml\n```\n\n**AMD / ROCm.** AMD setup is read-only diagnostic plus manual `.env` edit. Run:\n\n```bash\nscripts/check-docker-amd-gpu.sh\n```\n\nThen add the reported values to `.env`, replacing `RENDER_GID` with your host's\nnumeric render group id:\n\n```bash\nCOMPOSE_FILE=docker-compose.yml:docker/gpu.amd.yml\nRENDER_GID=989\n```\n\nFor NVIDIA/AMD GPU support, also read the comments in the selected overlay file: docker/gpu.nvidia.yml or docker/gpu.amd.yml.\n\n**Stack-management UIs (Portainer, Coolify, Dockhand, etc.).** These tools\noften accept only a single Compose file and do not reliably honor `COMPOSE_FILE`\nor multiple `-f` overlays. CLI users should keep using the `COMPOSE_FILE`\noverlay workflow above. For stack UIs, point the stack at one of the standalone\nfiles instead, which bundle the base stack plus the GPU settings:\n\n- `docker-compose.gpu-nvidia.yml` \u2014 still requires the NVIDIA Container Toolkit\n  on the host.\n- `docker-compose.gpu-amd.yml` \u2014 still requires host ROCm/kfd/DRI setup, the\n  `video`/`render` group membership, and `RENDER_GID` when needed.\n\nThe base `docker-compose.yml` plus the `docker/gpu.*.yml` overlays remain the\nsource of truth; the standalone files mirror them for single-file deployments.\n\nVerify after enabling either overlay:\n\n```bash\ndocker compose exec odysseus nvidia-smi -L   # NVIDIA\ndocker compose exec odysseus sh -lc 'test -e /dev/kfd && test -d /dev/dri && ls -l /dev/kfd /dev/dri/renderD*'  # AMD\n```\n\n> **GPU passthrough \u2260 llama.cpp CUDA.** `nvidia-smi` passing inside the\n> container confirms Docker GPU access, but llama.cpp also needs `cudart` and\n> the CUDA Toolkit at runtime. If Cookbook logs show `Unable to find cudart\n> library`, `Could NOT find CUDAToolkit`, `CUDA Toolkit not found`, or\n> tensors/layers assigned to CPU, that is a Cookbook/llama.cpp build issue \u2014\n> not a Docker passthrough failure. Re-install the serve engine via\n> **Cookbook \u2192 Dependencies** to get a CUDA-enabled build.\n>\n> The same split applies to AMD/ROCm: seeing `/dev/kfd` and `/dev/dri` inside\n> the container confirms device passthrough, not ROCm userspace or a\n> ROCm-enabled vLLM/llama.cpp build. `rocm-smi` and `rocminfo` are not expected\n> inside the slim Odysseus image.\n\n**Ollama with Docker.** If Ollama runs on the host, add this endpoint in\nSettings:\n\n```text\nhttp://host.docker.internal:11434/v1\n```\n\nOllama must listen outside its own loopback interface:\n\n```bash\nOLLAMA_HOST=0.0.0.0:11434 ollama serve\n```\n\nThis connects Odysseus in Docker to an Ollama server that is already running on\nyour host machine; it does not start Ollama inside the container.\n`host.docker.internal` is Docker's hostname for the host machine from inside the\ncontainer. Cookbook **Serve** is a separate workflow for serving downloaded\nmodels through Odysseus/llama.cpp, so Windows users with an existing Ollama\ninstall usually only need to add the endpoint in Settings.\n\n**Useful checks.**\n\n```bash\ndocker compose ps\ndocker compose logs --tail=120 odysseus\ndocker compose logs odysseus | grep -E 'ChromaDB|MemoryVectorStore|DEGRADED'\n```\n\n**macOS details.** `start-macos.sh` installs Homebrew deps, creates the venv,\nruns setup, and starts uvicorn on port `7860` because AirPlay often holds\n`7000`. It uses llama.cpp/Ollama for Metal. vLLM/SGLang are CUDA/ROCm-only and\ndo not run on macOS. MLX-only models are not served by Odysseus.\n\n</details>\n\n### Native Windows\n\n**One-command launcher** (creates the venv, installs deps, runs setup, starts the\nserver; safe to re-run):\n\n```powershell\ngit clone https://github.com/pewdiepie-archdaemon/odysseus.git\ncd odysseus\npowershell -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File .\\launch-windows.ps1\n```\n\nOr do it by hand:\n\n```powershell\ngit clone https://github.com/pewdiepie-archdaemon/odysseus.git\ncd odysseus\npy -3.11 -m venv venv\nvenv\\Scripts\\Activate.ps1\npip install -r requirements.txt\npython setup.py\npython -m uvicorn app:app --host 127.0.0.1 --port 7000\n```\n\nIf `python` points at an older interpreter, use `py -3.12` (or another installed\n3.11+ version) for the venv step.\n\n**Requirements:** Python 3.11+. The core app (chat, agent, memory, documents,\nemail, calendar, deep research) runs fully native. For full **Cookbook** background\nmodel downloads and the agent shell tool, also install\n[Git for Windows](https://git-scm.com/download/win) (provides `bash.exe`).\nLocal GPU *serving* of vLLM/SGLang needs Linux/WSL2; for a local model on Windows,\n[Ollama](https://ollama.com/download) is the easiest path \u2014 point Odysseus at\n`http://localhost:11434/v1` in Settings.\n\nOpen `http://localhost:7000`, log in with the generated admin password,\nand configure everything else inside **Settings**.\n\n## Troubleshooting & Advanced Setup\n\n### `chromadb-client` conflicts with embedded ChromaDB\nIf `chromadb-client` (the lightweight HTTP-only package) is installed alongside the full `chromadb` package, Odysseus starts but ChromaDB silently falls back to HTTP-only mode and fails.\n\n**Fix:** uninstall `chromadb-client` and force-reinstall the full package:\n```bash\n./venv/bin/pip uninstall chromadb-client -y\n./venv/bin/pip install --force-reinstall chromadb\n```\n\n### HTTPS + LAN/Tailscale exposure\nTo expose Odysseus on a local network or Tailscale with HTTPS:\n1. Change the bind address to `0.0.0.0` in `.env` (`APP_BIND=0.0.0.0` or `ODYSSEUS_HOST=0.0.0.0`).\n2. Generate a locally-trusted cert for your LAN/Tailscale IPs using [mkcert](https://github.com/FiloSottile/mkcert):\n   ```bash\n   mkcert -install\n   mkcert -cert-file cert.pem -key-file key.pem 192.168.1.100 tailscale-ip\n   ```\n3. Run `uvicorn` with the generated certs:\n   ```bash\n   python -m uvicorn app:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 7000 --ssl-certfile=cert.pem --ssl-keyfile=key.pem\n   ```\n4. Install the `mkcert` CA on any other device you want to access Odysseus from (e.g., for iOS, email the `rootCA.pem` to yourself, install the profile, and trust it in Certificate Trust Settings).\n\n### Optional Dependencies\n`requirements-optional.txt` contains packages that unlock extra features. It is not installed by default.\n\n| Package | Feature unlocked |\n|---------|-----------------|\n| `faster-whisper` | Local speech-to-text (microphone -> text) via the \"local\" STT provider. |\n| `duckduckgo-search` | DuckDuckGo as a search provider option. |\n| `PyMuPDF` | PDF page rendering in the side viewer panel and form-filling. (Note: AGPL-3.0) |\n| `markitdown` | Office/EPUB document text extraction (converts .docx/.xlsx/.pptx/.xls/.epub to Markdown). |\n\n## Security Notes\nOdysseus is a self-hosted workspace with powerful local tools: shell access, file uploads, model downloads, web research, email/calendar integrations, and API tokens. Treat it like an admin console.\n\n- Keep `AUTH_ENABLED=true` for any network-accessible deployment.\n- Keep `LOCALHOST_BYPASS=false` outside local development.\n- Use `SECURE_COOKIES=true` when Odysseus is served through HTTPS by a trusted reverse proxy or private access gateway.\n- Do not expose it directly to the public internet without HTTPS and a trusted reverse proxy or private access layer.\n- Keep `.env`, `data/`, `logs/`, databases, uploads, generated media, backups, auth/session files, API keys, and model/provider tokens out of Git and private shares. They are ignored by default.\n- Review `data/auth.json` after first boot: disable open signup unless you intentionally want it, make only your own account admin, and keep demo/test accounts non-admin.\n- Non-admin users do not get shell/Python/file read/write by default, and admin-only routes/tools such as MCP management, API tokens, webhooks, model/cookbook serving, backup/vault, and app settings are admin-gated. Other features are controlled by per-user privileges, so review each user's privileges before exposing a deployment.\n- Rotate any API keys or tokens that were ever pasted into a shared chat, demo, screenshot, or log.\n- If you enable API tokens or webhooks, create separate tokens per integration and delete unused ones.\n- Prefer binding manual development runs to `127.0.0.1`; bind to `0.0.0.0` only when you intentionally want LAN/reverse-proxy access.\n- Keep ChromaDB, SearXNG, ntfy, Ollama, vLLM, llama.cpp, databases, and raw model/provider APIs internal-only. Expose only the authenticated Odysseus web/API entrypoint through your trusted proxy or private access layer.\n- Before publishing a fork, run `git status --short` and confirm no private files from `.env`, `data/`, `logs/`, uploads, backups, or local databases are staged.\n\n### Private or proxied deployments\nOdysseus serves plain HTTP on its app port. Docker Compose binds Odysseus and the bundled services to `127.0.0.1` by default, so a typical production/private setup is:\n\n1. Keep Odysseus on localhost, for example `127.0.0.1:7000`.\n2. Terminate HTTPS at a trusted reverse proxy or private access gateway.\n3. Put the authenticated Odysseus web/API entrypoint behind that layer.\n4. Keep raw service and model ports internal-only.\n\nCloudflare Access, Tailscale, Caddy, nginx, and Traefik can all fit this pattern; none are required by Odysseus. If your access layer reaches Odysseus on the same host, proxy to `http://127.0.0.1:7000` and keep `AUTH_ENABLED=true`, `LOCALHOST_BYPASS=false`, and `SECURE_COOKIES=true`.\n\nCommon internal-only ports from the default docs/compose setup:\n\n| Port | Service |\n|---|---|\n| `7000` | Odysseus raw app port |\n| `8080` | SearXNG |\n| `8091` | ntfy |\n| `8100` | ChromaDB host port for manual/compose access |\n| `11434` | Ollama |\n| `8000-8020` | Common local model/provider APIs |\n\n## Contributing\nHelp is welcome. The best entry points are fresh-install testing, provider setup\nbugs, mobile/editor polish, docs, and small focused refactors. See\n[ROADMAP.md](ROADMAP.md) for the current help-wanted list.\n\n## Configuration\nMost setup is done inside the app with `/setup` or **Settings**. Use `.env`\nfor deployment-level defaults and secrets you want present before first boot.\nKey settings:\n\n| Variable | Default | Description |\n|---|---|---|\n| `LLM_HOST` | `localhost` | Your LLM server (e.g. `llm-host.local:8000`) |\n| `LLM_HOSTS` | -- | Comma-separated list for model discovery |\n| `OPENAI_API_KEY` | -- | Optional OpenAI key. Prefer adding providers in the app unless pre-seeding. |\n| `SEARXNG_INSTANCE` | `http://localhost:8080` | SearXNG URL. Docker overrides this to `http://searxng:8080`. |\n| `SEARXNG_SECRET` | generated on first Docker boot | Optional SearXNG cookie/CSRF secret. Leave blank unless you need to pin it. |\n| `APP_BIND` | `127.0.0.1` | Docker Compose host bind address for the web UI. Use `0.0.0.0` only for intentional LAN/reverse-proxy access. |\n| `APP_PORT` | `7000` | Docker Compose host port for the web UI. |\n| `AUTH_ENABLED` | `true` | Enable/disable login |\n| `LOCALHOST_BYPASS` | `false` | Development-only auth bypass for loopback requests. Keep false for shared/network deployments. |\n| `SECURE_COOKIES` | `false` | Set true when serving Odysseus through HTTPS at a trusted proxy or private access gateway. |\n| `DATABASE_URL` | `sqlite:///./data/app.db` | Database connection string |\n| `CHROMADB_HOST` | `localhost` | ChromaDB host for vector memory. Docker overrides this to `chromadb`. |\n| `CHROMADB_PORT` | `8100` | ChromaDB port for manual host runs. Docker overrides this to `8000`. |\n| `EMBEDDING_URL` | -- | OpenAI-compatible embeddings endpoint |\n\n### Built-in MCP servers (optional setup)\n\nOdysseus auto-registers a few built-in MCP servers at startup. The npx-based ones (currently the browser server, `@playwright/mcp`) only start when their npm package is already in the local npx cache. If a package isn't cached, that server is skipped with a startup log message explaining what to do, so a fresh install does not block on a multi-minute npm download or hang if Playwright system deps are missing.\n\nTo enable the browser MCP (page navigation, screenshots, vision), run once:\n\n```bash\nnpx -y @playwright/mcp@latest --version\n```\n\nThat installs `@playwright/mcp` plus Playwright (~300MB total). Restart Odysseus and the server will register at startup.\n\n## Architecture\n```\napp.py                   # FastAPI entry point\ncore/      auth, database, middleware, constants\nsrc/       llm_core, agent_loop, agent_tools, chat_processor, search/\nroutes/    chat, session, document, memory, model \u2026 endpoints\nservices/  docs, memory, search, hwfit (Cookbook) \u2026\nstatic/    index.html + app.js + style.css + js/ (modular front-end)\ndocs/      landing page (index.html) + preview clips\n```\n\n## Data\nAll user data lives in `data/` (gitignored): `app.db` (sessions, messages, documents),\n`memory.json`, `presets.json`, `uploads/`, `personal_docs/`, `chroma/`, `settings.json`.\n\n## Star History\n\n<a href=\"https://www.star-history.com/?repos=pewdiepie-archdaemon%2Fodysseus&type=date&legend=top-left\">\n <picture>\n   <source media=\"(prefers-color-scheme: dark)\" srcset=\"https://api.star-history.com/chart?repos=pewdiepie-archdaemon/odysseus&type=date&theme=dark&legend=top-left\" />\n   <source media=\"(prefers-color-scheme: light)\" srcset=\"https://api.star-history.com/chart?repos=pewdiepie-archdaemon/odysseus&type=date&legend=top-left\" />\n   <img alt=\"Star History Chart\" src=\"https://api.star-history.com/chart?repos=pewdiepie-archdaemon/odysseus&type=date&legend=top-left\" />\n </picture>\n</a>\n\n## License\nMIT -- see [LICENSE](LICENSE) and [ACKNOWLEDGMENTS.md](ACKNOWLEDGMENTS.md).\n\n```\n                                  |\n                                 |||\n                                |||||\n                  |    |    |   |||||||\n                 )_)  )_)  )_)   ~|~\n                )___))___))___)\\  |\n               )____)____)_____)\\\\|\n             _____|____|____|_____\\\\\\__\n             \\                       /\n       ~^~^~~^~^~~^~^~~^~^~~^~^~~^~^~~^~^~~^~^~\n               ~^~  all aboard!  ~^~\n       ~^~^~~^~^~~^~^~~^~^~~^~^~~^~^~~^~^~~^~^~\n```\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "4fd7f48c1a47b06cd9a077aff49407334a9995a3", "display_url": "https://github.com/pewdiepie-archdaemon/odysseus/blob/main/README.md", "display_title": "README.md"}
> ````
>
> Odysseus confirms the product target: self-hosted ChatGPT/Claude-style workspace, but its frontend is deliberately simpler: `static/index.html`, `static/app.js`, `static/style.css`, plus modular JS under `static/js`. I’m pulling those exact UI files now.
>
> I have enough local context; now I’ll fetch Odysseus’ static UI source files through GitHub.
>
> MCP tool call
>
> codex_apps.github_fetch_file
>
> ```json
> {
>   "repository_full_name": "pewdiepie-archdaemon/odysseus",
>   "path": "static/index.html",
>   "ref": "main",
>   "start_line": 1,
>   "end_line": 240
> }
> ```
>
> ```text
> {"content": "<!DOCTYPE html>\n<html lang=\"en\">\n<head>\n  <meta charset=\"UTF-8\" />\n  <title>Odysseus Chat</title>\n  <link rel=\"icon\" type=\"image/svg+xml\" href=\"data:image/svg+xml,%3Csvg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 32 32'%3E%3Cpath d='M16 4L16 22L6 22Z' fill='%23e06c75'/%3E%3Cpath d='M16 8L16 22L24 22Z' fill='%23e06c75' opacity='0.6'/%3E%3Cpath d='M4 24Q10 20 16 24Q22 28 28 24' stroke='%23e06c75' stroke-width='2.5' fill='none' stroke-linecap='round'/%3E%3C/svg%3E\">\n  <link rel=\"manifest\" href=\"/static/manifest.json\">\n  <meta name=\"theme-color\" content=\"#282c34\">\n  <meta name=\"apple-mobile-web-app-capable\" content=\"yes\">\n  <meta name=\"apple-mobile-web-app-status-bar-style\" content=\"black-translucent\">\n  <!-- Disable iOS / Android auto-linkifying of numbers (phone/date/address)\n       in email bodies \u2014 was wrapping random digits in <a href=\"tel:...\"> with\n       browser-default styling that didn't match the Odysseus theme. -->\n  <meta name=\"format-detection\" content=\"telephone=no, date=no, address=no, email=no\">\n  <link rel=\"apple-touch-icon\" href=\"/static/icon-192.png\">\n  <script nonce=\"{{CSP_NONCE}}\">\n  window._odysseusLoadTime = Date.now();\n  (function(){\n    try {\n      var t = JSON.parse(localStorage.getItem('odysseus-theme'));\n      if (t && t.colors) {\n        var s = document.documentElement.style;\n        var c = t.colors;\n        s.setProperty('--bg', c.bg);\n        s.setProperty('--fg', c.fg);\n        s.setProperty('--panel', c.panel);\n        s.setProperty('--border', c.border);\n        if (c.red) s.setProperty('--red', c.red);\n        // Set --brand-color now (= custom brand or the theme red) so the\n        // loading-screen ASCII wave shows its final colour from the first\n        // paint. Otherwise it starts on --red and visibly switches once the\n        // app boots and applyColors() fills --brand-color.\n        var _bc = (c.advanced && c.advanced.brandColor) || c.red;\n        if (_bc) s.setProperty('--brand-color', _bc);\n        // Match the mobile browser toolbar / status bar to the saved theme bg\n        // from the very first paint. Otherwise the hardcoded dark meta sits\n        // there during load and the bar \"switches\" colour once the app boots.\n        var mtc = document.querySelector('meta[name=\"theme-color\"]');\n        if (mtc && c.bg) mtc.setAttribute('content', c.bg);\n        // Update favicon to match accent color\n        var ac = c.red || '#e06c75';\n        var fav = document.querySelector(\"link[rel='icon']\");\n        if (fav) fav.href = 'data:image/svg+xml,' + encodeURIComponent(\"<svg xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg' viewBox='0 0 32 32'><path d='M16 4L16 22L6 22Z' fill='\" + ac + \"'/><path d='M16 8L16 22L24 22Z' fill='\" + ac + \"' opacity='0.6'/><path d='M4 24Q10 20 16 24Q22 28 28 24' stroke='\" + ac + \"' stroke-width='2.5' fill='none' stroke-linecap='round'/></svg>\");\n        // Derive syntax highlighting colors from theme\n        function h2hsl(hex) {\n          hex = hex.replace('#','');\n          var r=parseInt(hex.substring(0,2),16)/255, g=parseInt(hex.substring(2,4),16)/255, b=parseInt(hex.substring(4,6),16)/255;\n          var mx=Math.max(r,g,b), mn=Math.min(r,g,b), h, sv, l=(mx+mn)/2;\n          if(mx===mn){h=sv=0}else{var d=mx-mn;sv=l>0.5?d/(2-mx-mn):d/(mx+mn);if(mx===r)h=((g-b)/d+(g<b?6:0))/6;else if(mx===g)h=((b-r)/d+2)/6;else h=((r-g)/d+4)/6}\n          return[h*360,sv*100,l*100];\n        }\n        function hsl2h(h,sv,l) {\n          h=((h%360)+360)%360;sv=Math.max(0,Math.min(100,sv))/100;l=Math.max(0,Math.min(100,l))/100;\n          var a=sv*Math.min(l,1-l);function f(n){var k=(n+h/30)%12;return l-a*Math.max(-1,Math.min(k-3,9-k,1))}\n          function th(v){return Math.round(v*255).toString(16).padStart(2,'0')}\n          return'#'+th(f(0))+th(f(8))+th(f(4));\n        }\n        var fH=h2hsl(c.fg),bH=h2hsl(c.bg),rH=h2hsl(c.red||'#e06c75');\n        var dk=bH[2]<50;\n        s.setProperty('--hl-bg', hsl2h(bH[0],bH[1],dk?Math.max(bH[2]-4,0):Math.min(bH[2]+4,100)));\n        s.setProperty('--hl-fg', c.fg);\n        s.setProperty('--hl-keyword', hsl2h((rH[0]+280)%360,Math.min(rH[1]+10,80),dk?70:45));\n        s.setProperty('--hl-string', hsl2h(40,Math.min(fH[1]+20,70),dk?72:42));\n        s.setProperty('--hl-comment', hsl2h(fH[0],Math.max(fH[1]-20,5),(fH[2]*0.5+bH[2]*0.5)));\n        s.setProperty('--hl-function', hsl2h(210,Math.min(fH[1]+20,75),dk?70:45));\n        s.setProperty('--hl-number', hsl2h(20,Math.min(fH[1]+15,65),dk?68:48));\n        s.setProperty('--hl-builtin', hsl2h(180,Math.min(fH[1]+15,60),dk?65:40));\n        s.setProperty('--hl-variable', hsl2h((fH[0]+30)%360,Math.min(fH[1]+5,60),fH[2]));\n        s.setProperty('--hl-params', hsl2h(fH[0],Math.max(fH[1]-5,10),dk?Math.min(fH[2]+8,85):Math.max(fH[2]-8,25)));\n        // Apply advanced overrides if present\n        if (c.advanced) {\n          var a = c.advanced;\n          var advMap = {userBubbleBg:'--user-bubble-bg',aiBubbleBg:'--ai-bubble-bg',bubbleBorder:'--bubble-border',sidebarBg:'--sidebar-bg',sectionAccent:'--section-accent',brandColor:'--brand-color',inputBg:'--input-bg',inputBorder:'--input-border',sendBtnBg:'--send-btn-bg',sendBtnHover:'--send-btn-hover',codeBg:'--code-bg',codeFg:'--code-fg',toggleBg:'--toggle-bg',toggleActive:'--toggle-active',accentPrimary:'--accent-primary',accentError:'--accent-error'};\n          for (var k in advMap) { if (a[k]) s.setProperty(advMap[k], a[k]); }\n        }\n      }\n      // Apply font early\n      if (t && t.font) {\n        var fm = {mono:\"'Fira Code', monospace\",sans:\"system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', sans-serif\",serif:\"Georgia, 'Times New Roman', serif\"};\n        if (fm[t.font]) { s.setProperty('--font-family', fm[t.font]); }\n        else { s.setProperty('--font-family', \"'\" + t.font.replace(/'/g,'') + \"', sans-serif\"); }\n      }\n      // Apply density class on html\n      if (t && t.density && t.density !== 'comfortable') {\n        document.documentElement.classList.add('density-' + t.density);\n      }\n      // Apply background pattern on body once available\n      if (t && t.bgPattern && t.bgPattern !== 'none') {\n        document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', function() {\n          document.body.classList.add('bg-pattern-' + t.bgPattern);\n        }, {once:true});\n      }\n    } catch(e){}\n  })();\n  </script>\n  <!-- Per-route favicon. Bookmarking /calendar shows a calendar icon,\n       /notes a notes icon, etc. Each shape is rendered in the current\n       theme accent color. document.querySelector(\"link[rel='icon']\");\n      if (!fav) { fav = document.createElement('link'); fav.rel = 'icon'; fav.type = 'image/svg+xml'; document.head.appendChild(fav); }\n      fav.href = = titles[path];\n      // Per-route Android home-screen icon. We swap the <link rel=\"manifest\">\n      // to a per-page Blob URL with this route's SVG icon \u2014 that way \"Add to\n      // Home Screen\" picks up the route-specific glyph instead of the shared\n      // boat logo. Falls back silently on browsers without Blob URL support.\n      try {\n        if (inner && typeof Blob !== 'undefined') {\n          var pwa = {\n            name: (titles[path] || 'Odysseus'),\n            short_name: (titles[path] || 'Odysseus').split('\u2014')[0].trim(),\n            start_url: path,\n            scope: '/',\n            display: 'standalone',\n            background_color: '#0e0e10',\n            theme_color: ac,\n            icons: [\n              { src: href, sizes: '192x192', type: 'image/svg+xml', purpose: 'any maskable' },\n              { src: href, sizes: '512x512', type: 'image/svg+xml', purpose: 'any maskable' },\n            ],\n          };\n          var blob = new Blob([JSON.stringify(pwa)], { type: 'application/manifest+json' });\n          var url = URL.createObjectURL(blob);\n          var ml = document.querySelector(\"link[rel='manifest']\");\n          if (!ml) { ml = document.createElement('link'); ml.rel = 'manifest'; document.head.appendChild(ml); }\n          ml.href = url;\n        }\n      } catch(_) {}\n    } catch(e){}\n  })();\n  </script>\n  <script defer src=\"/static/lib/highlight.min.js\"></script>\n  <!-- Inter font \u2014 self-hosted, no Google dependencies -->\n  <style>\n    @font-face { font-family: 'Inter'; font-weight: 400; font-style: normal; font-display: swap; src: url('/static/fonts/Inter-Regular.woff2') format('woff2'); }\n    @font-face { font-family: 'Inter'; font-weight: 500; font-style: normal; font-display: swap; src: url('/static/fonts/Inter-Medium.woff2') format('woff2'); }\n    @font-face { font-family: 'Inter'; font-weight: 600; font-style: normal; font-display: swap; src: url('/static/fonts/Inter-SemiBold.woff2') format('woff2'); }\n  </style>\n  <!-- KaTeX CSS is loaded with media=\"print\" so it doesn't block render,\n       then flipped to \"all\" via JS after load. Mermaid init runs once the\n       library finishes loading. Both hooks are wired via addEventListener\n       below (inline onload= attrs are blocked by CSP script-src-attr). -->\n  <link id=\"katex-css\" rel=\"stylesheet\" href=\"https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.22/dist/katex.min.css\" media=\"print\">\n  <script async src=\"https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/katex@0.16.22/dist/katex.min.js\"></script>\n  <script id=\"mermaid-script\" async src=\"https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/mermaid@11/dist/mermaid.min.js\"></script>\n  <script nonce=\"{{CSP_NONCE}}\">\n    (function(){\n      var k = document.getElementById('katex-css');\n      if (k) k.addEventListener('load', function(){ k.media = 'all'; }, { once: true });\n      var m = document.getElementById('mermaid-script');\n      if (m) m.addEventListener('load', function(){\n        if (window.odysseusInitMermaid) window.odysseusInitMermaid();\n      }, { once: true });\n    })();\n  </script>\n  <link rel=\"stylesheet\" href=\"/static/style.css\">\n  <link rel=\"modulepreload\" href=\"/static/app.js\">\n  <link rel=\"modulepreload\" href=\"/static/js/chat.js\">\n  <link rel=\"modulepreload\" href=\"/static/js/ui.js\">\n  <link rel=\"modulepreload\" href=\"/static/js/sessions.js\">\n  <link rel=\"modulepreload\" href=\"/static/js/markdown.js\">\n  <meta name=\"viewport\" content=\"width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0, interactive-widget=resizes-content, viewport-fit=cover\" />\n</head>\n<body>\n  <!-- Loading overlay \u2014 hides all flashing until app is ready -->\n  <div id=\"app-loader\" style=\"position:fixed;inset:0;z-index:99999;background:var(--bg,#282c34);display:flex;align-items:center;justify-content:center;flex-direction:column;gap:8px;transition:opacity .3s\">\n    <div id=\"loader-wave\" style=\"color:var(--brand-color,var(--red,#e06c75));font-family:monospace;font-size:11px;opacity:.5\">\u2581\u2582\u2583</div>\n  </div>\n  <script nonce=\"{{CSP_NONCE}}\">\n  (function(){\n    var el=document.getElementById('loader-wave');\n    if(!el)return;\n    var frames=['\u2581\u2582\u2583','\u2582\u2583\u2584','\u2583\u2584\u2585','\u2584\u2585\u2586','\u2585\u2586\u2585','\u2586\u2585\u2584','\u2585\u2584\u2583','\u2584\u2583\u2582','\u2583\u2582\u2581'];\n    var i=0;\n    var iv=setInterval(function(){\n      if(!document.getElementById('app-loader')){clearInterval(iv);return}\n      el.textContent=frames[i%frames.length];\n      i++;\n    },150);\n > {"content": "// ============================================\n// Odysseus UI \u2014 Main Application Orchestrator\n// ES6 module \u2014 entry point, no exports (wires all modules together)\n// ============================================\nimport Storage from './js/storage.js';\nimport uiModule from './js/ui.js';\nimport fileHandlerModule from './js/fileHandler.js';\nimport modelsModule from './js/models.js';\nimport ragModule from './js/rag.js';\nimport presetsModule from './js/presets.js';\nimport searchModule from './js/search.js';\nimport chatModule from './js/chat.js';\nimport compareModule from './js/compare/index.js';\nimport documentModule from './js/document.js';\nimport searchChatModule from './js/search-chat.js';\nimport markdownModule from './js/markdown.js';\nimport chatRenderer from './js/chatRenderer.js';\nimport sessionModule from './js/sessions.js';\nimport memoryModule from './js/memory.js';\nimport voiceRecorderModule from './js/voiceRecorder.js';\nimport censorModule from './js/censor.js';\nimport galleryModule from './js/gallery.js';\nimport tasksModule from './js/tasks.js';\nimport calendarModule from './js/calendar.js';\nimport notesModule from './js/notes.js';\nimport adminModule from './js/admin.js';\nimport settingsModule from './js/settings.js';\n// Eagerly bind unified minimize/restore behavior across all tool modals.\nimport './js/modalManager.js';\n// Desktop window tiling \u2014 drag a modal near an edge/corner to snap.\nimport './js/tileManager.js';\nimport themeModule from './js/theme.js';\n// IMPORTANT: import cookbook.js with NO ?v= query \u2014 the same plain specifier\n// every other importer (cookbook-hwfit.js / cookbook-diagnosis.js) uses. A query\n// mismatch makes the browser load cookbook.js twice as separate modules (two\n// _envState objects), which broke server selection. Keep all cookbook imports\n// unversioned so this can't recur.\nimport cookbookModule from './js/cookbook.js';\nimport groupModule from './js/group.js';\nimport * as researchPanelModule from './js/research/panel.js';\nimport ttsModule from './js/tts-ai.js';\nimport spinnerModule from './js/spinner.js';\nimport { initKeyboardShortcuts } from './js/keyboard-shortcuts.js';\nimport { initSidebarLayout, syncRailSide } from './js/sidebar-layout.js';\nimport { initSectionCollapse, initSectionDrag } from './js/section-management.js';\n\nconst API_BASE = window.location.origin;\nwindow.themeModule = themeModule;\nwindow.sessionModule = sessionModule;\nwindow.uiModule = uiModule;\nwindow.adminModule = adminModule;\nwindow.cookbookModule = cookbookModule;\n\n// Redirect to login on 401 from any fetch\nconst _origFetch = window.fetch;\nwindow.fetch = async function(...args) {\n  const res = await _origFetch.apply(this, args);\n  if (res.status === 401 && !String(args[0]).includes('/api/auth/')) {\n    window.location.href = '/login';\n  }\n  return res;\n};\n\n// Search settings\n\n\nconst el = uiModule.el;\n\n// Default chat config \u2014 refreshed on every new-chat action so settings\n// changes take effect immediately (previously cached once at page load and\n// went stale when the user changed their default model).\nlet _defaultChat = null;\nasync function _refreshDefaultChat() {\n  try {\n    const d = await (await fetch('/api/default-chat')).json();\n    if (d && d.endpoint_url && d.model) {\n      _defaultChat = d;\n      try { window.__odysseusDefaultChat = d; } catch (_) {}\n      return d;\n    }\n  } catch (_) {}\n  return null;\n}\n// Prime the cache once at load for initial paint paths that read _defaultChat\n// synchronously; later reads should call _refreshDefaultChat() first.\n_refreshDefaultChat();\n\nasync function _createDirectChatFromPreferredModel() {\n  if (!sessionModule) return false;\n\n  const pending = sessionModule.getPendingChat && sessionModule.getPendingChat();\n  if (pending && pending.url && pending.modelId) {\n    sessionModule.createDirectChat(pending.url, pending.modelId, pending.endpointId);\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  const sessions = sessionModule.getSessions();\n  const currentId = sessionModule.getCurrentSessionId();\n  const current = sessions.find(s => s.id === currentId);\n  if (current && current.endpoint_url && current.model) {\n    sessionModule.createDirectChat(current.endpoint_url, current.model, current.endpoint_id);\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  const dc = await _refreshDefaultChat();\n  if (dc) {\n    sessionModule.createDirectChat(dc.endpoint_url, dc.model, dc.endpoint_id);\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  const withModel = sessions.filter(s => s.endpoint_url && s.model);\n  if (withModel.length > 0) {\n    const last = withModel[0]; // sessions are sorted by recent\n    sessionModule.createDirectChat(last.endpoint_url, last.model, last.endpoint_id);\n    return true;\n  }\n\n  return false;\n}\n\n// ============================================\n// EVENT LISTENERS INITIALIZATION\n// ============================================\nfunction initializeEventListeners() {\n  // Chat form submission\n//  document.getElementById('chat-form').addEventListener('submit', chatModule.handleChatSubmit);\n\n  // File attachments (inside overflow menu)\n  const _overflowAttach = el('overflow-attach-btn');\n  if (_overflowAttach) _overflowAttach.addEventListener('click', fileHandlerModule.openPicker);\n  el('file-input').addEventListener('change', (e)=>{\n    for (const f of e.target.files) fileHandlerModule.addFiles([f]);\n    fileHandlerModule.renderAttachStrip();\n    // Refocus textarea after file picker closes (mobile keyboard)\n    const ta = el('message');\n    if (ta) setTimeout(() => ta.focus(), 100);\n  });\n\n  // Paste handler\n  window.addEventListener('paste', async (e)=>{\n    if (!e.clipboardData) return;\n    let changed = false;\n    for (const item of e.clipboardData.items){\n      if (item.kind === 'file'){\n        const f = item.getAsFile();\n        if (f) {\n          fileHandlerModule.addFiles([f]);\n          changed = true;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n    if (changed) fileHandlerModule.renderAttachStrip();\n  });\n\n  // Message count in the header \u2014 recount on any DOM change in\n  // #chat-history and write \"\u00b7 N msgs\" next to the title. Counts top-\n  // level .msg elements (one per user/assistant turn); excludes the\n  // welcome screen since it isn't inside chat-history.\n  const _metaCountEl = el('current-meta-count');\n  const _chatHistEl = el('chat-history');\n  if (_metaCountEl && _chatHistEl) {\n    let _countScheduled = false;\n    const _updateMsgCount = () => {\n      _countScheduled = false;\n      const n = _chatHistEl.querySelectorAll(':scope > .msg').length;\n      _metaCountEl.textContent = n ? `\u00b7 ${n} msg${n === 1 ? '' : 's'}` : '';\n    };\n    const _scheduleCount = () => {\n      if (_countScheduled) return;\n      _countScheduled = true;\n      requestAnimationFrame(_updateMsgCount);\n    };\n    new MutationObserver(_scheduleCount).observe(_chatHistEl, { childList: true });\n    _updateMsgCount();\n  }\n\n  // Scrolling\n  el('chat-history').addEventListener('scroll', uiModule.debounce(() => {\n    const box = el('chat-history');\n    const atBottom = box.scrollHeight - box.scrollTop - box.clientHeight < 80;\n    uiModule.setAutoScroll(atBottom);\n  }, 100));\n  // Close all footer popups immediately on any scroll\n  el('chat-history').addEventListener('scroll', () => {\n    document.querySelectorAll('.ctx-popup, .memory-used-detail, .msg-overflow-menu').forEach(p => p.remove());\n    document.querySelectorAll('.memory-used-pill').forEach(p => { p._openDetail = null; });\n  }, { passive: true });\n\n  el('chat-history').addEventListener('wheel', (e) => {\n    // Only disable auto-scroll when user scrolls UP (deltaY < 0)\n    if (e.deltaY < 0) uiModule.setAutoScroll(false);\n  });\n  let _touchThrottled = false;\n  el('chat-history').addEventListener('touchmove', () => {\n    if (_touchThrottled) return;\n    _touchThrottled = true;\n    uiModule.setAutoScroll(false);\n    requestAnimationFrame(() => { _touchThrottled = false; });\n  }, { passive: true });\n\n  // Internal #session-id links from AI search results\n  el('chat-history').addEventListener('click', (e) => {\n    const link = e.target.closest('a.chat-link');\n    if (!link) return;\n    const href = link.getAttribute('href');\n    if (href && href.startsWith('#') && sessionModule) {\n      e.preventDefault();\n      sessionModule.selectSession(href.slice(1));\n    }\n  });\n\n  // Export dropdown button\n  const exportDlBtn = el('export-dl-btn');\n  // \u2500\u2500 Unified popup dismissal \u2500\u2500\n  // Lightweight popups (header dropdowns, kebab menus, pickers) should vanish\n  // on any \"other action\" \u2014 opening the sidebar, opening a tool window, etc.\n  // Each popup used to wire its own outside-click/Escape close but missed\n  // non-click actions. closeAllPopups() centralizes it: toggled menus drop\n  // their `.open`; ephemeral body-appended menus are removed. Full modals/\n  // windows are deliberately NOT touched here \u2014 those close via their own", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "683e0e5532c4f9033bac5dcc69aeed207bd15e21", "display_url": "https://github.com/pewdiepie-archdaemon/odysseus/blob/main/static/app.js", "display_title": "app.js"}
> ```

> {"content": "// static/js/ui.js\n\n/**\n * UI utilities for toasts, modals, scrolling, and user feedback\n */\n\nimport themeModule from './theme.js';\nimport * as Modals from './modalManager.js';\nimport spinnerModule from './spinner.js';\nimport { registerMenuDismiss, dismissTopMenu, dismissOrRemove } from './escMenuStack.js';\n\nlet toastEl = null;\nlet autoScrollEnabled = true;\nlet hoveredToggleCard = null;\nlet hoveredToggleWindow = null;\nlet hoveredDockChip = null;\nlet _lastPointerClientX = null;\nlet _lastPointerClientY = null;\n\n// Smooth scroll state\nlet _scrollRafId = null;\nlet _scrollBox = null;\n\nfunction _isTextEditingTarget(target) {\n  const el = target && target.nodeType === 1 ? target : target?.parentElement;\n  return !!(el && el.closest('input, textarea, select, [contenteditable=\"true\"], [contenteditable=\"\"]'));\n}\n\nfunction _targetEl(target) {\n  return target && target.nodeType === 1 ? target : target?.parentElement || null;\n}\n\nconst SPACE_CARD_SELECTOR = [\n  '#email-lib-modal .doclib-card',\n  '#doclib-modal .doclib-card',\n  '#doclib-modal .doclib-chat-row',\n  '#memory-modal .doclib-card',\n  '#tasks-modal .task-card',\n  '#tasks-modal .task-log-row',\n  '#research-overlay [data-job-id]',\n  '#cookbook-modal .doclib-card',\n  '.email-reader-tab-modal .doclib-card',\n  '.email-window-modal .doclib-card',\n].join(', ');\n\nconst SPACE_BLOCKED_SELECTOR = [\n  'button',\n  'a',\n  'input',\n  'textarea',\n  'select',\n  '[contenteditable=\"true\"]',\n  '[contenteditable=\"\"]',\n  '.recipient-chip',\n  '.doclib-card-dropdown',\n  '.email-card-dropdown',\n  '.task-log-row-actions',\n  '.modal-header',\n].join(', ');\n\nfunction _visibleModalForSpace(win) {\n  const modal = win?.closest?.('.modal[id]');\n  if (!modal || modal.classList.contains('hidden') || modal.classList.contains('modal-minimized')) return null;\n  return modal;\n}\n\nfunction _isSpaceVisible(el) {\n  if (!el || !document.contains(el)) return false;\n  if (el.closest?.('.modal.hidden, .modal.modal-minimized, [hidden]')) return false;\n  return true;\n}\n\nfunction _spaceWindowId(win) {\n  if (!win || !document.contains(win)) return null;\n  const modal = _visibleModalForSpace(win);\n  if (modal && Modals.isRegistered(modal.id)) return modal.id;\n  if (win.closest?.('.doc-editor-pane') && Modals.isRegistered('doc-panel') && !Modals.isMinimized('doc-panel')) return 'doc-panel';\n  return null;\n}\n\nfunction _windowAtPointer() {\n  if (_lastPointerClientX == null || _lastPointerClientY == null) return null;\n  const x = _lastPointerClientX;\n  const y = _lastPointerClientY;\n  const candidates = [\n    ...document.querySelectorAll('.modal:not(.hidden):not(.modal-minimized) .modal-content'),\n    ...document.querySelectorAll('.doc-editor-pane'),\n  ].filter(el => {\n    if (!document.contains(el)) return false;\n    const r = el.getBoundingClientRect();\n    return x >= r.left && x <= r.right && y >= r.top && y <= r.bottom;\n  });\n  if (!candidates.length) return null;\n  return candidates.reduce((top, el) => {\n    const mz = parseInt(getComputedStyle(el.closest('.modal') || el).zIndex, 10) || 0;\n    const tz = parseInt(getComputedStyle(top.closest('.modal') || top).zIndex, 10) || 0;\n    return mz >= tz ? el : top;\n  });\n}\n\nfunction _containsPointer(el) {\n  if (!el || _lastPointerClientX == null || _lastPointerClientY == null) return false;\n  const r = el.getBoundingClientRect();\n  return _lastPointerClientX >= r.left && _lastPointerClientX <= r.right\n    && _lastPointerClientY >= r.top && _lastPointerClientY <= r.bottom;\n}\n\nfunction _closeHoveredWindow() {\n  let win = _windowAtPointer();\n  if (!win) {\n    try {\n      const underPointer = document.elementFromPoint(_lastPointerClientX, _lastPointerClientY);\n      win = underPointer?.closest?.('.modal:not(.hidden):not(.modal-minimized) .modal-content, .doc-editor-pane') || null;\n    } catch {}\n  }\n  if (!win) win = hoveredToggleWindow;\n  if (!win || !document.contains(win)) return false;\n  const modalForWin = win.closest?.('.modal[id]');\n  if (modalForWin?.id === 'email-lib-modal') {\n    const closeBtn = document.getElementById('email-lib-close') || modalForWin.querySelector('.close-btn');\n    if (closeBtn) {\n      try { closeBtn.click(); return true; } catch {}\n    }\n    try { modalForWin.remove(); return true; } catch {}\n  }\n  const id = _spaceWindowId(win);\n  if (id && Modals.isRegistered(id)) {\n    Modals.close(id);\n    return true;\n  }\n  const modal = _visibleModalForSpace(win);\n  if (!modal) return false;\n  const closeBtn = modal.querySelector('.close-btn, .modal-close, .modal-close-btn, [data-action=\"close\"]');\n  if (closeBtn) {\n    try { closeBtn.click(); return true; } catch {}\n  }\n  try { modal.classList.add('hidden'); return true; } catch {}\n  return false;\n}\n\nfunction _spaceIsBlocked(e, surface) {\n  const target = _targetEl(e.target);\n  if (!target) return false;\n  if (_isTextEditingTarget(target)) return !surface || surface.contains(target);\n  const blocked = target.closest?.(SPACE_BLOCKED_SELECTOR);\n  return !!(blocked && (!surface || surface.contains(blocked)));\n}\n\nfunction _activateSpaceCard(card) {\n  if (!card || !document.contains(card)) return false;\n  if (card.matches('#tasks-modal .task-card')) {\n    const titleRow = card.querySelector('.memory-item-title')?.closest('div');\n    if (titleRow) {\n      titleRow.click();\n      return true;\n    }\n  }\n  card.dataset.spaceToggle = '1';\n  card.click();\n  setTimeout(() => {\n    try { delete card.dataset.spaceToggle; } catch {}\n  }, 0);\n  return true;\n}\n\nfunction _initHoverCardSpaceToggle() {\n  if (document._odysseusHoverCardSpaceToggle) return;\n  document._odysseusHoverCardSpaceToggle = true;\n  document.addEventListener('pointerover', (e) => {\n    _lastPointerClientX = e.clientX;\n    _lastPointerClientY = e.clientY;\n    const chip = e.target?.closest?.('.minimized-dock-chip[data-modal-id]');\n    if (chip) hoveredDockChip = chip;\n    const card = e.target?.closest?.(SPACE_CARD_SELECTOR);\n    if (card) hoveredToggleCard = card;\n    const win = e.target?.closest?.('.modal:not(.hidden):not(.modal-minimized) .modal-content, .doc-editor-pane');\n    if (win) hoveredToggleWindow = win;\n  }, true);\n  document.addEventListener('pointermove', (e) => {\n    _lastPointerClientX = e.clientX;\n    _lastPointerClientY = e.clientY;\n  }, true);\n  document.addEventListener('pointerout', (e) => {\n    const next = e.relatedTarget;\n    if (hoveredDockChip && (!next || !hoveredDockChip.contains(next))) hoveredDockChip = null;\n    if (hoveredToggleCard && (!next || !hoveredToggleCard.contains(next))) hoveredToggleCard = null;\n    if (hoveredToggleWindow && (!next || !hoveredToggleWindow.contains(next))) hoveredToggleWindow = null;\n  }, true);\n  document.addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {\n    if (e.code !== 'Space' || e.repeat) return;\n    if (hoveredToggleCard && _isSpaceVisible(hoveredToggleCard)) {\n      if (_spaceIsBlocked(e, hoveredToggleCard)) return;\n      e.preventDefault();\n      _activateSpaceCard(hoveredToggleCard);\n      return;\n    }\n    if (hoveredDockChip && document.contains(hoveredDockChip)) {\n      if (_spaceIsBlocked(e, hoveredDockChip)) return;\n      const id = hoveredDockChip.dataset.modalId;\n      if (id && Modals.isRegistered(id)) {\n        e.preventDefault();\n        Modals.restore(id);\n      }\n      return;\n    }\n    const id = _spaceWindowId(hoveredToggleWindow);\n    if (!id) return;\n    if (_spaceIsBlocked(e, hoveredToggleWindow)) return;\n    e.preventDefault();\n    Modals.minimize(id);\n  }, true);\n}\n\n_initHoverCardSpaceToggle();\n\n/**\n * Copy text to clipboard\n */\nexport async function copyToClipboard(text) {\n  try {\n    await navigator.clipboard.writeText(text);\n    showToast('Copied');\n  }\n  catch {\n    const ta = document.createElement('textarea');\n    ta.value = text;\n    ta.style.cssText = 'position:fixed;left:-9999px;top:-9999px;opacity:0';\n    document.body.appendChild(ta);\n    ta.select();\n    document.execCommand('copy');\n    document.body.removeChild(ta);\n    showToast('Copied');\n  }\n}\n\n// Wire swipe-to-dismiss on the shared toast element. Runs once, the first\n// time a toast is shown. Tracks horizontal touch drag; if the user drags\n// more than DISMISS_PX, the toast slides off in the drag direction and\n// hides early. Anything less snaps back. Desktop unaffected (touch\n// listeners only fire from a touchscreen \u2014 mouse is handled by the\n// existing \u00d7 button and auto-hide timer).\nfunction _wireToastSwipe(el) {\n  if (!el || el._swipeWired) return;\n  el._swipeWired = true;\n  const DISMISS_PX = 70;\n  let startX = 0, currentX = 0, swiping = false;\n  el.addEventListener('touchstart', (e) => {\n    if (!el.classList.contains('show')) return;\n    const t = e.touches[0];\n    if (!t) return;\n    startX = t.clientX;\n    currentX = t.clientX;\n    swiping = true;\n    // Kill the slide-in transition mid-flight so the touch tracks the\n    // finger 1:1 instead of fighting a still-running animation.\n    el.style.transition = 'none';\n  }, { passive: true });\n  el.addEventListener('touchmove', (e) => {\n    if (!swiping) return;\n    const t = e.touches[0];", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "aa82cc61640cc207ff8c79a192fdc6fd8255474d", "display_url": "https://github.com/pewdiepie-archdaemon/odysseus/blob/main/static/js/ui.js", "display_title": "ui.js"}

class=\"memory-toolbar-btn\" title=\"Test every skill, auto-fix the weak ones, flag what still fails\"><svg width=\"11\" height=\"11\" viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\" fill=\"currentColor\" class=\"skills-audit-panel hidden\"></div>\n            <div id=\"skills-bulk-bar\" class=\"memory-bulk-bar hidden\">\n              <label class=\"memory-bulk-check-all\" style=\"position:relative;top:0px;\"><input type=\"checkbox\" id=\"skills-select-all\" /> All</label>\n              <span id=\"skills-selected-count\">0 Selected</span>\n              <button id=\"skills-bulk-publish\" class=\"memory-toolbar-btn\" disabled title=\"Publish selected drafts\" style=\"margin-left:auto;\"><svg width=\"11\" height=\"11\" viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\" fill=\"none\" >
> A useful thing I’ve already learned: Odysseus is not “pretty dashboard cards.” Its strength is app behavior and shell polish: early theme boot, loader, dense chat/session ergonomics, floating/focused tools, and custom CSS variables. Rasputin should borrow that product architecture, not the clutterier deep modal internals.
>
> <details><summary>Called 4 tools</summary>
>
> MCP tool call

>

> {"content": "// static/js/chat.js\n\n/**\n * Main chat functionality - message handling and streaming\n */\n// ES6 module \u2014 IIFE removed\n\nimport Storage from './storage.js';\nimport uiModule from './ui.js';\nimport sessionModule from './sessions.js';\nimport chatRenderer from './chatRenderer.js';\nimport chatStream from './chatStream.js';\nimport { addAITTSButton } from './tts-ai.js';\nimport markdownModule from './markdown.js';\nimport spinnerModule from './spinner.js';\nimport presetsModule from './presets.js';\nimport fileHandlerModule from './fileHandler.js';\nimport searchModule from './search.js';\nimport documentModule from './document.js';\nimport * as emailInbox from './emailInbox.js';\nimport codeRunnerModule from './codeRunner.js';\nimport slashCommands, { initSlashCommands, isCommand, handleSlashCommand, handleSetupInput, handleSetupWizard, typewriterInto } from './slashCommands.js';\nimport createResearchSynapse from './researchSynapse.js';\n  const RESEARCH_TIMEOUT_MS = 360000;\n  const DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MS = 120000;\n  const RESEARCH_SVG = '<svg width=\"16\" height=\"16\" viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\"><circle cx=\"11\" cy=\"11\" r=\"8\"/><path d=\"M21 21l-4.35-4.35\"/></svg>';\n\n  let API_BASE = '';\n  let currentAbort = null;\n  let isStreaming = false;\n  // Continuous stall watchdog: while streaming, if the SSE stream produces\n  // NOTHING for STALL_THRESHOLD_MS (no deltas, no tool heartbeat \u2014 tools beat\n  // every 2s, so a full minute of silence means it's genuinely stuck or the\n  // model quietly stopped), surface a non-destructive \"still working?\" prompt\n  // instead of silently hanging. Replaces relying only on the tab-refocus\n  // recovery (which fired only on visibilitychange and silently reloaded).\n  let _stallWatchdog = null;\n  let _stallBannerShown = false;\n  const STALL_THRESHOLD_MS = 60000;\n  let _sendInFlight = false;   // covers the window from click \u2192 streaming start\n  let _displayOverride = null; // Override visible user bubble text (hides injected prompts)\n  let _hideUserBubble = false; // Skip user bubble entirely (e.g. continue after stop)\n  let _pendingContinue = null; // Stores the stopped AI element to merge with new response\n  // \u2500\u2500 Auto-recovery: when a turn's stream silently dies (connection drop) or\n  // goes quiet while the connection is alive, re-engage the model with a\n  // completion handshake instead of leaving it hung. Capped so it can't loop.\n  let _autoNudges = 0;             // handshakes fired for the CURRENT user turn\n  let _autoContinuePending = false; // marks the next submit as an auto-continue (don't reset the counter)\n  const _AUTO_NUDGE_CAP = 3;\n\n  // shortModel and modelColor are now in chatRenderer.js\n  var _shortModel = chatRenderer.shortModel;\n  var _applyModelColor = chatRenderer.applyModelColor;\n  // Per-session research tracking (supports concurrent research across sessions)\n  const _researchingStreamIds = new Set();\n  let _researchTimerEl = null, _researchTimerInterval = null;\n  let _researchStartTime = 0, _researchAvgDuration = null;\n  let _researchSynapse = null;\n  function _clearResearchTimer() {\n    if (_researchTimerInterval) { clearInterval(_researchTimerInterval); _researchTimerInterval = null; }\n    if (_researchTimerEl) { _researchTimerEl.remove(); _researchTimerEl = null; }\n    if (_researchSynapse) {\n      // Mark complete first so the user briefly sees the \"done\" state,\n      // then tear it down on next tick.\n      try { _researchSynapse.complete(); } catch {}\n      const s = _researchSynapse;\n      _researchSynapse = null;\n      setTimeout(() => { try { s.destroy(); } catch {} }, 800);\n    }\n    _researchStartTime = 0;\n    _researchAvgDuration = null;\n  }\n\n  /** Append a \"Generate Visual Report\" button \u2014 delegates to chatRenderer. */\n  function _appendViewReportLink(msgEl, sessionId) {\n    const body = msgEl.querySelector('.body');\n    if (body) chatRenderer.appendReportButton(body, sessionId);\n  }\n  let currentAccumulated = ''; // Track accumulated text across function scope\n  let currentHolder = null; // Track current message holder\n  let currentSpinner = null; // Track current spinner for stop cleanup\n\n  // Background streaming support\n  const _backgroundStreams = new Map(); // sessionId -> { status, accumulated, sourcesHtml, abortCtrl, query, metrics }\n  let _streamSessionId = null; // Session ID for the currently active reader loop\n  let _lastReaderActivity = 0; // Timestamp of last reader.read() success \u2014 used to detect frozen streams\n  let _webLockRelease = null;  // Function to release the Web Lock held during streaming\n\n  /** Check if an SSE reader is still actively connected for a session. */\n  function hasActiveStream(sessionId) {\n    return _streamSessionId === sessionId || _backgroundStreams.has(sessionId);\n  }\n\n  // Sources box builder and toggleSources are now in chatRenderer.js\n  var _buildSourcesBox = chatRenderer.buildSourcesBox;\n\n  // Browser notifications now in chatStream.js\n  var _notifyResearchComplete = chatStream.notifyResearchComplete;\n\n  // Model/image pricing, _buildImageBubble now in chatRenderer.js\n  var _buildImageBubble = chatRenderer.buildImageBubble;\n  var getModelCost = chatRenderer.getModelCost;\n  var getImageCost = chatRenderer.getImageCost;\n\n  // stripToolBlocks and roleTimestamp now in chatRenderer.js\n  var stripToolBlocks = chatRenderer.stripToolBlocks;\n\n  function _normalizeEndpointForCompare(url) {\n    if (!url) return '';\n    try {\n      const u = new URL(String(url), window.location.origin);\n      let path = u.pathname.replace(/\\/+$/, '');\n      const suffixes = [\n        '/v1/chat/completions', '/chat/completions',\n        '/v1/completions', '/completions',\n        '/v1/messages', '/messages',\n        '/v1/models', '/models',\n      ];\n      for (const suffix of suffixes) {\n        if (path.toLowerCase().endsWith(suffix)) {\n          path = path.slice(0, -suffix.length).replace(/\\/+$/, '');\n          break;\n        }\n      }\n      return (u.origin + path).toLowerCase();\n    } catch (_) {\n      return String(url).trim().replace(/\\/+$/, '').toLowerCase();\n    }\n  }\n\n  async function _probeCurrentEndpointStatus(endpointUrl, signal) {\n    const target = _normalizeEndpointForCompare(endpointUrl);\n    if (!target) return null;\n    const modelsRes = await fetch(`${API_BASE}/api/models`, { credentials: 'same-origin', signal });\n    if (!modelsRes.ok) return null;\n    const modelsData = await modelsRes.json().catch(() => ({}));\n    const item = (modelsData.items || []).find(ep =>\n      _normalizeEndpointForCompare(ep.url || ep.endpoint_url || ep.base_url) === target\n    );\n    if (!item || !item.endpoint_id) return null;\n\n    const probesRes = await fetch(`${API_BASE}/api/model-endpoints/probe-local`, {\n      credentials: 'same-origin',\n      signal,\n    });\n    if (!probesRes.ok) return null;\n    const probes = await probesRes.json().catch(() => ({}));\n    return probes[item.endpoint_id] || null;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Initialize with dependencies\n   */\n  export function init(apiBase) {\n    API_BASE = apiBase;\n    initSlashCommands({ apiBase, isStreaming: () => isStreaming });\n    // Initialize email inbox\n    emailInbox.init(documentModule);\n    // Wire the slash-command autocomplete popup on the chat composer. The\n    // dispatcher already handles the typed command \u2014 this just surfaces the\n    // registry as a discoverable menu when the user starts a message with /.\n    import('./slashAutocomplete.js').then(mod => {\n      const ta = document.getElementById('message');\n      if (ta && mod.initSlashAutocomplete) mod.initSlashAutocomplete(ta);\n    }).catch(() => {});\n  }\n\n  // addMessage, createMsgFooter, displayMetrics, hideWelcomeScreen, showWelcomeScreen\n  // are now in chatRenderer.js \u2014 referenced via the public API delegation above.\n  var addMessage = chatRenderer.addMessage;\n  var createMsgFooter = chatRenderer.createMsgFooter;\n  var displayMetrics = chatRenderer.displayMetrics;\n  var hideWelcomeScreen = chatRenderer.hideWelcomeScreen;\n  var showWelcomeScreen = chatRenderer.showWelcomeScreen;\n\n  /**\n   * Update submit button state\n   */\n  function updateSubmitButton(state, submitBtn) {\n    if (!submitBtn) return;\n\n    if (state === 'streaming') {\n      // Clear any pending transitions from + \u2192 arrow swap\n      submitBtn.classList.remove('anim-spin', 'anim-spin-swap', 'anim-land', 'mic-mode', 'newchat-mode', 'newchat-expanded', 'recording');\n      // Ensure arrow icon is showing before launch\n      var icons = window._odysseusBtnIcons;\n      if (icons) submitBtn.innerHTML = icons.send;\n      void submitBtn.offsetWidth;\n      // Arrow launches up, then stop icon lands in\n      submitBtn.classList.add('anim-launch');\n      const _stopSvg = '<svg width=\"14\" height=\"14\" viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\" fill=\"currentColor\"><rect x=\"6\" y=\"6\" width=\"12\" height=\"12\" rx=\"2\"/></svg>';\n      // Wait for the launch keyframe to finish (0.3s) before swapping the\n      // arrow out for the stop icon \u2014 otherwise the swap happens mid-flight\n      // and the user sees nothing fly out.\n      setTimeout(() => {\n        submitBtn.innerHTML = _stopSvg;\n        submitBtn.classList.remove('anim-launch');\n        void submitBtn.offsetWidth;\n        submitBtn.classList.add('anim-land');\n        submitBtn.addEventListener('animationend', () => submitBtn.classList.remove('anim-land'), { once: true });\n      }, 300);\n      submitBtn.title = 'Stop generation';\n      submitBtn.dataset.mode = 'streaming';\n      submitBtn.dataset.phase = 'processing';\n      isStreaming = true;\n      _startStallWatchdog();\n    } else if (state === 'idle') {\n      submitBtn.dataset.mode = '';\n      delete submitBtn.dataset.phase;\n      submitBtn.classList.remove('recording');\n      isStreaming = false;\n      _stopStallWatchdog();\n      // Defer to global updater which handles mic/newchat/send modes\n      if (window._updateSendBtnIcon) {\n        setTimeout(window._updateSendBtnIcon, 50);\n      } else {\n        var icons = window._odysseusBtnIcons;\n        submitBtn.innerHTML = icons ? icons.send : '<svg width=\"16\" height=\"16\" viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2.5\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\"><path d=\"M12 19V5M5 12l7-7 7 7\"/></svg>';\n        submitBtn.title = 'Send message';\n        submitBtn.classList.remove('mic-mode', 'newchat-mode');\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n  // Slash commands \u2014 now in slashCommands.js\n  // -----------------------------------------------------------------------\n\n  // API key pattern for the guard in handleChatSubmit\n  const API_KEY_RE = /^(sk-[a-zA-Z0-9_\\-]{20,}|gsk_[a-zA-Z0-9]{20,}|AIza[a-zA-Z0-9_\\-]{30,}|xai-[a-zA-Z0-9]{20,})$/;\n\n\n  /**\n   * Handle chat form submission\n   */\n  export async function handleChatSubmit(e) {\n    e.preventDefault();\n    // Cancel research clarification timeout if active\n    if (window._researchTimeoutTimer) {\n      clearTimeout(window._researchTimeoutTimer);\n      window._researchTimeoutTimer = null;\n    }\n    // Get current session\n    const sessionId = sessionModule.getCurrentSessionId();\n    const session = sessionModule.getSessions().find(s => s.id === sessionId);\n    \n    const submitBtn = document.querySelector('.send-btn');\n    \n    // If compare is active, stop all compare streams\n    if (window.compareModule && window.compareModule.isActive()) {\n      window.compareModule.handleCompareSubmit();\n      return;\n    }\n\n    // If currently streaming, stop it\n    if (isStreaming) {\n      // Cancel server-side research if in progress\n      const _cancelSid = sessionModule.getCurrentSessionId();\n      if (_cancelSid && _researchingStreamIds.has(_cancelSid)) {\n        fetch(`${API_BASE}/api/research/cancel/${_cancelSid}`, { method: 'POST' }).catch(e => console.warn('Research cancel failed:', e));", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha":
