without this, a touch-drag on the canvas pans\\n     the page / sheet instead of drawing (\\\"the whole window moves\\\"). */\\n  touch-action: none;\\n}\\n.sig-modal-overlay .sig-name,\\n.sig-modal-overlay .sig-smoothness {\\n  font-family: inherit;\\n}\\n.sig-modal-overlay .sig-name {\\n  width: 100%;\\n  padding: 6px 8px;\\n  border: 1px solid var(--border);\\n  background: var(--bg);\\n  color: var(--fg);\\n  border-radius: 4px;\\n  font-size: 0.85rem;\\n}\\n.sig-modal-overlay .sig-tile {\\n  position: relative;\\n  border: 1px solid var(--border);\\n  border-radius: 6px;\\n  padding: 8px;\\n  cursor: pointer;\\n  background: var(--bg);\\n}\\n.sig-modal-overlay .sig-tile:hover { border-color: var(--fg); }\\n.sig-modal-overlay .sig-tile img {\\n  display: block;\\n  width: 100%;\\n  height: 80px;\\n  object-fit: contain;\\n  background: #fff;\\n  border-radius: 3px;\\n}\\n.sig-modal-overlay .sig-tile-del {\\n  position: absolute;\\n  top: 1px;\\n  right: 6px;\\n  width: 20px;\\n  height: 20px;\\n  /* Center the \\u00d7 glyph inside the round bg \\u2014 without this the button's default\\n     text metrics push it past the right edge of the circle. */\\n  display: flex;\\n  align-items: center;\\n  justify-content: center;\\n  padding: 0 0 0 1px;\\n  box-sizing: border-box;\\n  font-size: 0.85rem;\\n  line-height: 1;\\n  border: 1px solid var(--accent, var(--red));\\n  background: #fff;\\n  color: var(--accent, var(--red));\\n  border-radius: 50%;\\n  cursor: pointer;\\n  opacity: 1;\\n}\\n.sig-modal-overlay .sig-tile-del:hover {\\n  background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--accent, var(--red)) 10%, #fff);\\n}\\n.sig-modal-overlay .sig-new-tile {\\n  border: 2px dashed var(--border);\\n  border-radius: 6px;\\n  padding: 8px;\\n  cursor: pointer;\\n  background: var(--bg);\\n  color: var(--fg);\\n  display: flex;\\n  align-items: center;\\n  justify-content: center;\\n  min-height: 106px;\\n  font-weight: 600;\\n  opacity: 0.85;\\n}\\n.sig-modal-overlay .sig-new-tile:hover {\\n  opacity: 1;\\n  border-color: var(--fg);\\n}\\n\\n/* Form checkboxes \\u2014 render as a circular dot, theme-aware. Used in the\\n   Export PDF modal and the inline PDF view overlay. */\\n.pdf-export-overlay input[type=\\\"checkbox\\\"],\\n#doc-pdf-view input[type=\\\"checkbox\\\"] {\\n  -webkit-appearance: none;\\n  appearance: none;\\n  margin: 0;\\n  cursor: pointer;\\n  border-radius: 50%;\\n  background: #fff;\\n  position: relative;\\n}\\n.pdf-export-overlay input[type=\\\"checkbox\\\"] {\\n  width: 14px;\\n  height: 14px;\\n  border: 1.5px solid var(--border);\\n  background: var(--bg);\\n}\\n.pdf-export-overlay input[type=\\\"checkbox\\\"]:checked {\\n  background: var(--fg);\\n  border-color: var(--fg);\\n}\\n/* PDF view overlays on a white page render \\u2014 keep colors theme-independent\\n   so the dot is always visible against the rendered PDF. */\\n#doc-pdf-view input[type=\\\"checkbox\\\"] {\\n  border: 1.5px solid #444;\\n}\\n#doc-pdf-view input[type=\\\"checkbox\\\"]:checked {\\n  background: #111;\\n  border-color: #111;\\n}\\n\\n/* \\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\n   Frosted glass theme \\u2014 applied when the user enables the \\\"Glass\\\" toggle\\n   in theme settings (writes `body.theme-frosted`). Layers a semi-translucent\\n   tint over every panel/modal/sidebar/dropdown and blurs whatever is\\n   behind it, so the UI reads like layered glass.\\n   \\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500\\u2500 */\\nbody.theme-frosted #sidebar,\\nbody.theme-frosted .modal-content,\\nbody.theme-frosted .doclib-modal-content,\\nbody.theme-frosted .gallery-modal-content,\\nbody.theme-frosted .notes-pane,\\nbody.theme-frosted .research-pane,\\nbody.theme-frosted .calendar-pane,\\nbody.theme-frosted .cookbook-pane,\\nbody.theme-frosted .doc-pane,\\nbody.theme-frosted .doc-pane-floating,\\nbody.theme-frosted .cp-popover,\\nbody.theme-frosted .dropdown,\\nbody.theme-frosted .overflow-menu,\\nbody.theme-frosted .doc-tab-dropdown,\\nbody.theme-frosted .email-reader,\\nbody.theme-frosted .admin-card,\\nbody.theme-frosted .gallery-detail-menu,\\nbody.theme-frosted #theme-popup .modal-content {\\n  /* Tinted base color (set separately so the shorthand can't swallow it);\\n     much more transparent than before so what's behind actually shows\\n     through. The blur catches whatever pixels are below. */\\n  background-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--panel, var(--bg)) 32%, transparent) !important;\\n  background-image: linear-gradient(180deg,\\n    color-mix(in srgb, var(--fg, #fff) 14%, transparent) 0%,\\n    color-mix(in srgb, var(--fg, #fff)  4%, transparent) 26%,\\n    transparent 55%) !important;\\n  backdrop-filter: blur(24px) saturate(170%) !important;\\n  -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(24px) saturate(170%) !important;\\n  border-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--fg) 22%, transparent) !important;\\n}\\n/* The sidebar header + user bar paint their own opaque --sidebar-bg/--panel.\\n   Under the frosted theme the sidebar is translucent glass, so those solid\\n   bands stood out as a dark (near-black) strip over the title/avatar area.\\n   Make them transparent so they blend into the frosted sidebar. */\\nbody.theme-frosted .sidebar-header,\\nbody.theme-frosted .sidebar-user-bar {\\n  background: transparent !important;\\n}\\n/* Same problem on tool modals: the sticky .modal-header paints a solid\\n   var(--panel) bar (so scrolled content doesn't bleed through). Over a\\n   frosted-glass modal body that solid bar reads as a dark band across the\\n   title area (Calendar, Tasks, Cookbook, Memory, Email\\u2026). Give the header\\n   its own matching frosted glass \\u2014 translucent tint + blur \\u2014 so it stays\\n   readable while scrolling but blends into the panel instead of a black band. */\\nbody.theme-frosted .modal-header {\\n  /* Transparent \\u2014 NOT another panel tint. The header sits on top of the\\n     already-frosted modal body, so any added tint stacks and the band\\n     reads darker than its surroundings. Keep just the blur (which doesn't\\n     darken) so scrolled content stays legible under the sticky header. */\\n  background-color: transparent !important;\\n  background-image: none !important;\\n  backdrop-filter: blur(24px) !important;\\n  -webkit-backdrop-filter: blur(24px) !important;\\n}\\n/* Deep Research's header isn't sticky (content doesn't scroll under it) and\\n   its pane carries extra frosting \\u2014 saturate + a top gradient highlight +\\n   the synapse glow. The generic header blur above adds its OWN glass layer,\\n   which renders a different shade than the pane behind it. Drop the header's\\n   blur so the pane's frosted background shows through the header uniformly. */\\nbody.theme-frosted .research-pane-header {\\n  background-color: transparent !important;\\n  background-image: none !important;\\n  backdrop-filter: none !important;\\n  -webkit-backdrop-filter: none !important;\\n}\\nbody.theme-frosted .notes-mobile-grabber {\\n  background-color: transparent !important;\\n  background-image: none !important;\\n  backdrop-filter: none !important;\\n  -webkit-backdrop-filter: none !important;\\n}\\n/* Inner admin-cards inside frosted modals stack their transparency on top\\n   of the modal's, which kills the see-through. Make the inner card nearly\\n   fully transparent so the outer modal's blur dominates. */\\nbody.theme-frosted .modal-content .admin-card,\\nbody.theme-frosted .doclib-modal-content .admin-card,\\nbody.theme-frosted .gallery-modal-content .admin-card,\\nbody.theme-frosted #theme-popup .admin-card {\\n  background-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--panel, var(--bg)) 10%, transparent) !important;\\n  background-image: none !important;\\n  backdrop-filter: none !important;\\n  -webkit-backdrop-filter: none !important;\\n}\\n/* Subtle inner light highlight + softer shadow on the heavier surfaces */\\nbody.theme-frosted .modal-content,\\nbody.theme-frosted .doclib-modal-content,\\nbody.theme-frosted .gallery-modal-content,\\nbody.theme-frosted .notes-pane,\\nbody.theme-frosted .research-pane,\\nbody.theme-frosted .calendar-pane,\\nbody.theme-frosted .cookbook-pane,\\nbody.theme-frosted .doc-pane,\\nbody.theme-frosted .doc-pane-floating {\\n  box-shadow:\\n    0 14px 36px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.5),\\n    inset 0 1px 0 color-mix(in srgb, var(--fg, #fff) 14%, transparent),\\n    inset 0 -1px 0 color-mix(in srgb, #000 14%, transparent) !important;\\n}\\n/* Backdrops should be MUCH less opaque so the blur effect reads through to\\n   the actual page content behind the modal. */\\nbody.theme-frosted .modal {\\n  background: color-mix(in srgb, #000 12%, transparent) !important;\\n}\\n\\n/* Mobile only: lift the Dependencies \\\"Server\\\" dropdown's selected text up ~2px. */\\n@media (max-width: 768px) {\\n  #hwfit-deps-server { padding-bottom: 4px; line-height: 1; }\\n}\\n\\n/* Emoji rendered as monochrome line icons (OpenMoji-black via /api/emoji proxy)\\n   instead of colorful system glyphs \\u2014 project rule: never colorful emoji. The\\n   SVG is used as a mask filled with the current text color, so it tints to the\\n   theme. Sized to sit on the text baseline. */\\n.emoji {\\n  height: 1.15em;\\n  width: 1.15em;\\n  vertical-align: -0.18em;\\n  margin: 0 0.05em;\\n  display: inline-block;\\n  background-color: currentColor;\\n  -webkit-mask: var(--em) center / contain no-repeat;\\n          mask: var(--em) center / contain no-repeat;\\n}\\n\\n/* Nudge the X icon in the \\\"Clear all\\\" button down 2px. */\\n#research-clear-all svg { position: relative; top: 2px; }\\n\\n/* RESEARCH SCROLL FORCE \\u2014 guarantee the jobs list scrolls inside the bounded\\n   pane (the pane is height-capped: 85vh desktop, 100dvh mobile). Each ancestor\\n   needs min-height:0 for a nested flex scroll to engage. */\\n#research-pane { min-height: 0; }\\n/* The BODY is the single scroller \\u2014 the list flows into it and the whole body\\n   scrolls. (A nested list-scroller kept clipping instead of scrolling.) */\\n#research-pane .research-pane-body {\\n  flex: 1 1 auto !important; min-height: 0 !important;\\n  display: flex !important; flex-direction: column !important;\\n  overflow-y: auto !important; -webkit-overflow-scrolling: touch;\\n}\\n#research-pane .research-jobs-list {\\n  flex: 0 0 auto !important; min-height: 0 !important;\\n  overflow: visible !important; max-height: none !important;\\n}\\n\\n/* RESEARCH FULLSCREEN MOBILE \\u2014 the pane wasn't opening to full height on mobile,\\n   so its content overflowed with nowhere to scroll. Pin it to the full viewport\\n   (id selector + !important beats the inherited modal-content sizing). */\\n@media (max-width: 768px) {\\n  /* Bottom-sheet: 90dvh tall (a touch smaller than full-screen) anchored to the\\n     bottom, so there's a small gap at the top and it reads as a sheet. The body\\n     still scrolls within the bounded height. */\\n  #research-overlay { align-items: flex-end !important; justify-content: stretch !important; }\\n  #research-pane {\\n    height: 90dvh !important;\\n    max-height: 90dvh !important;\\n    width: 100vw !important;\\n    max-width: 100vw !important;\\n    border-radius: 14px 14px 0 0 !important;\\n  }\\n  /* Bottom breathing room so the expanded Settings + Start button clear the\\n     browser's bottom toolbar / safe area and can always be scrolled into view\\n     (otherwise the form fits the viewport and there's no room to reach them). */\\n  #research-pane .research-pane-body {\\n    padding-bottom: calc(env(safe-area-inset-bottom, 0px) + 40px) !important;\\n    touch-action: pan-y;\\n    overscroll-behavior: contain;\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n/* Quick fade + up-nudge. */\\n.research-start-btn { position: relative; top: -6px; transition: opacity 0.16s ease; }\\n\\n/* Right-side group of the section header: clear button (past), status dot, chevron. */\\n.research-section-right { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 8px; margin-left: auto; }\\n/* \\\"Clear all\\\" in the Past header \\u2014 styled like the cookbook-running clear btn. */\\n.research-section-clear {\\n  font-size: 10px; padding: 0 8px; height: 22px;\\n  border-radius: 6px; border: 1px solid var(--border);\\n  color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--fg) 45%, transparent);\\n  background: none; cursor: pointer; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 4px;\\n  font-family: inherit; white-space: nowrap; transition: all 0.15s;\\n  position: relative; top: -2px;\\n}\\n.research-section-clear:hover { color: var(--red); border-color: var(--red); background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--red) 8%, transparent); }\\n.research-section-clear svg { width: 11px; height: 11px; }\\n\\n/* Visual Report button tinted with the research type colour (--cat-color),\\n   falling back to the accent for \\\"standard\\\" (uncategorized) research. */\\n.research-job-action.research-job-action-report {\\n  color: var(--cat-color, var(--accent, var(--red)));\\n  border-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--cat-color, var(--accent, var(--red))) 45%, var(--border));\\n}\\n.research-job-action.research-job-action-report:hover {\\n  color: var(--cat-color, var(--accent, var(--red)));\\n  border-color: var(--cat-color, var(--accent, var(--red)));\\n  background: color-mix(in srgb, var(--cat-color, var(--accent, var(--red))) 10%, transparent);\\n}\\n\\n/* Nudge the \\\"+ Queue\\\" button up 5px. */\\n#research-add-btn { position: relative; top: -5px; }\\n/* Nudge the Start play-icon and Queue plus-sign up 1px so they sit visually\\n   centered with the button label text (their glyph baselines drop low). */\\n.research-start-btn svg { position: relative; top: -1px; }\\n.research-add-plus { position: relative; top: -1px; display: inline-block; }\\n\\n/* Footer hint under Past research linking to the Library's Research tab. */\\n.research-library-hint {\\n  /* full-width line in the header, pulled up with negative MARGIN (collapses\\n     the gap so it moves up without making the header taller). */\\n  width: 100%; flex-basis: 100%; margin: -22px 0 0; line-height: 1.2;\\n}\\n.research-library-link {\\n  background: none; border: none; padding: 0; cursor: pointer;\\n  font: inherit; color: var(--accent, var(--red)); text-decoration: underline;\\n}\\n.research-library-link:hover { opacity: 0.8; }\\n\\n/* Nudge the Delete button 4px left. */\\n.research-job-action[data-action=\\\"delete\\\"] { position: relative; right: 2px; }\\n\\n/* \\\"Standard\\\" (uncategorized) research uses green everywhere \\u2014 set its --cat-color\\n   to the success green so the Visual Report button matches the green badge. */\\n.research-job-card.done:not([data-category]) { --cat-color: var(--color-success); }\\n\\n/* Failed research (0 sources) \\u2014 red flag + actionable note. */\\n.research-job-card.research-job-failed { border-color: color-mix(in srgb, #f44336 40%, var(--border)); }\\n.research-cat-badge.research-cat-failed { color: #f44336; display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 3px; }\\n.research-cat-badge.research-cat-failed svg { width: 10px; height: 10px; }\\n.research-job-failnote { font-size: 11px; color: #f44336; opacity: 0.9; margin: 4px 0 6px; line-height: 1.35; }\\n\\n/* Shared popup-menu row feedback. Many small menus use their own item class;\\n   keep the hover light and consistent instead of giving one action a special\\n   glow. */\\n:is(\\n  .overflow-menu-item,\\n  .dropdown-item,\\n  .dropdown-item-compact,\\n  .export-dropdown-item,\\n  .sort-dropdown-item,\\n  .note-reminder-menu-item,\\n  .research-run-mode-popover .research-run-mode-row,\\n  .ge-fx-menu-item\\n) {\\n  transition:\\n    background-color 0.14s ease,\\n    color 0.14s ease,\\n    opacity 0.14s ease,\\n    transform 0.14s ease;\\n}\\n:is(\\n  .overflow-menu-item,\\n  .dropdown-item,\\n  .dropdown-item-compact,\\n  .export-dropdown-item,\\n  .sort-dropdown-item,\\n  .note-reminder-menu-item,\\n  .research-run-mode-popover .research-run-mode-row,\\n  .ge-fx-menu-item\\n):hover:not(:disabled) {\\n  opacity: 1;\\n  color: var(--fg);\\n  background-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--accent, var(--red)) 10%, transparent);\\n  transform: translateX(1px);\\n}\\n\\n/* Slash command autocomplete popup, anchored to the message composer */\\n.slash-autocomplete-popup {\\n  position: fixed;\\n  z-index: 9000;\\n  background: var(--panel, var(--bg));\\n  border: 1px solid var(--border, rgba(255,255,255,0.08));\\n  border-radius: 8px;\\n  box-shadow: 0 8px 24px rgba(0,0,0,0.35);\\n  font-size: 13px;\\n  color: var(--fg, #e6e6e6);\\n  overflow-y: auto;\\n  padding: 4px 0;\\n  display: none;\\n}\\n.slash-ac-cat {\\n  font-size: 10px;\\n  letter-spacing: 0.08em;\\n  text-transform: uppercase;\\n  color: var(--fg-muted, #888);\\n  padding: 6px 10px 2px;\\n  opacity: 0.7;\\n}\\n.slash-ac-row {\\n  display: flex;\\n  align-items: baseline;\\n  gap: 8px;\\n  padding: 5px 10px;\\n  cursor: pointer;\\n  line-height: 1.3;\\n  white-space: nowrap;\\n  overflow: hidden;\\n}\\n.slash-ac-row:hover { background-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--accent, var(--red)) 10%, transparent); }\\n.slash-ac-row-sel  { background-color: color-mix(in srgb, var(--accent, var(--red)) 14%, transparent); }\\n.slash-ac-token {\\n  font-family: 'Fira Code', ui-monospace, monospace;\\n  color: var(--accent, var(--red));\\n  font-weight: 600;\\n  flex-shrink: 0;\\n}\\n.slash-ac-help {\\n  color: var(--fg);\\n  opacity: 0.85;\\n  flex: 1;\\n  min-width: 0;\\n  overflow: hidden;\\n  text-overflow: ellipsis;\\n}\\n.slash-ac-usage {\\n  color: var(--fg-muted, #888);\\n  font-family: 'Fira Code', ui-monospace, monospace;\\n  font-size: 11px;\\n  opacity: 0.55;\\n  flex-shrink: 0;\\n}\\n.slash-ac-empty {\\n  padding: 10px;\\n  color: var(--fg-muted, #888);\\n  font-style: italic;\\n}\\n.slash-ac-empty code {\\n  font-family: 'Fira Code', ui-monospace, monospace;\\n  color: var(--accent, var(--red));\\n}\\n\\n/* \\u2550\\u2550 iOS focus-zoom fix \\u2014 touch devices only; desktop sizes untouched \\u2550\\u2550\\n   16px is the threshold below which iOS Safari auto-zooms on focus.\\n   Selects and date/time inputs are excluded on purpose \\u2014 they open native\\n   pickers and never zoom. */\\n@media (hover: none) and (pointer: coarse) {\\n\\n  /* 1 \\u2500\\u2500 Catch-all: every text-entry control NOT pinned with its own\\n     !important. !important here beats any non-important rule regardless of\\n     specificity, so this clears the long tail (settings, admin, memory,\\n     notes, calendar, email, gallery, tasks, model picker, etc.). */\\n  input[type=\\\"text\\\"],\\n  input[type=\\\"search\\\"],\\n  input[type=\\\"email\\\"],\\n  input[type=\\\"url\\\"],\\n  input[type=\\\"tel\\\"],\\n  input[type=\\\"password\\\"],\\n  input[type=\\\"number\\\"],\\n  input:not([type]),\\n  textarea {\\n    font-size: 16px !important;\\n  }\\n\\n  /* 2 \\u2500\\u2500 Fields that pin their own !important at specificity our catch-all\\n     can't beat. Each is matched at equal-or-higher specificity and, being\\n     later in the file, wins the tie. */\\n  #message { font-size: 16px !important; }                                  /* chat composer (was 13px !important) */\\n  .cookbook-dl-repo,\\n  .hwfit-search { font-size: 16px !important; }                             /* cookbook repo path + hardware search */\\n  .ge-topbar input { font-size: 16px !important; }                          /* image-editor topbar input */\\n  .ge-transform-field > input.ge-transform-popup-input {                    /* image-editor transform values */\\n    font-size: 16px !important;\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n@media (hover: none) and (pointer: coarse) {\\n  /* Only the sub-16px tiers need bumping; large lands ABOVE 16 so it\\n     stays zoom-safe AND visibly larger than medium (otherwise L collapses\\n     onto M on touch). All three editor layers move together so the\\n     highlight/line-number overlay stays metrically aligned with the textarea. */\\n  .doc-font-m .doc-editor-textarea, .doc-font-m .doc-editor-highlight, .doc-font-m .doc-line-numbers {\\n    font-size: 16px !important;   /* was 13px */\\n  }\\n  .doc-font-l .doc-editor-textarea, .doc-font-l .doc-editor-highlight, .doc-font-l .doc-line-numbers {\\n    font-size: 18px !important;   /* was 15px \\u2014 keep L > M */\\n  }\\n  /* Email compose rich-body. Medium (15px) zooms, so bump it; large (17px)\\n     is already \\u226516px and never zoomed \\u2014 leave it so we don't shrink it. */\\n  .doc-email-richbody.doc-font-m { font-size: 16px !important; }\\n}\\n\"}"}],"isError":false}
> ```
>
> </details>
>
> I’m also checking Rasputin’s current shell and dependency shape so the plan doesn’t ask for a rewrite that fights the app we already have. The goal is to replace the feel, not throw away the working backend/runtime pieces.

> {"content": "// ============================================\n// Sidebar Layout \u2014 icon rail, hamburger cycling, mobile backdrop & swipe\n// ============================================\n\nlet _syncRailSideFn = null;\n\n/**\n * Get the current syncRailSide function reference.\n * Needed because it gets patched after initial setup.\n */\nexport function syncRailSide() {\n  if (_syncRailSideFn) _syncRailSideFn();\n}\n\n/**\n * Initialize sidebar layout: icon rail, hamburger cycling, mobile backdrop, swipe gestures.\n * @param {Object} Storage - Storage module\n * @param {Object} opts\n * @param {Object} opts.documentModule - Document module (for swapSide)\n * @param {Function} opts._closeCompareIfActive\n * @param {Function} opts._deactivateIncognito\n * @param {Object} opts.presetsModule\n * @param {Object} opts.sessionModule\n * @param {Function} opts.el - Element lookup helper\n * @param {*} opts._defaultChat - Default chat config\n * @param {Function} opts._syncResearchIndicator\n */\nexport function initSidebarLayout(Storage, opts) {\n  const {\n    documentModule, _closeCompareIfActive, _deactivateIncognito,\n    presetsModule, sessionModule, el, _defaultChat, _syncResearchIndicator\n  } = opts;\n\n  // \u2500\u2500 Icon rail + sidebar toggle \u2500\u2500\n  const iconRail = document.getElementById('icon-rail');\n  const hamburgerBtn = document.getElementById('hamburger-btn');\n\n  function _syncRailSideCore() {\n    const sidebar = document.getElementById('sidebar');\n    if (!iconRail) return;\n    const isRight = sidebar.classList.contains('right-side');\n    const sidebarHidden = sidebar.classList.contains('hidden');\n    const railHidden = iconRail.classList.contains('rail-hidden');\n    const isMobileMini = iconRail.classList.contains('mobile-mini');\n    iconRail.classList.toggle('right-side', isRight);\n    // On mobile mini mode, JS already set inline styles \u2014 don't touch\n    if (isMobileMini) {\n      // Just update side positioning\n      if (isRight) {\n        iconRail.style.left = 'auto';\n        iconRail.style.right = '0';\n      } else {\n        iconRail.style.left = '0';\n        iconRail.style.right = 'auto';\n      }\n    } else {\n      iconRail.style.display = (sidebarHidden && !railHidden) ? '' : 'none';\n    }\n    // Hamburger is always visible \u2014 just update body classes for CSS layout adjustments\n    if (hamburgerBtn) {\n      document.body.classList.toggle('hamburger-right', isRight);\n      document.body.classList.toggle('hamburger-left', !isRight);\n      document.body.classList.toggle('hamburger-only', sidebarHidden && railHidden);\n      document.body.classList.toggle('sidebar-collapsed', sidebarHidden);\n    }\n    // Keep incognito button clear of hamburger\n    const incogBtn = document.getElementById('incognito-btn');\n    if (incogBtn) {\n      if (isRight && sidebarHidden) {\n        incogBtn.style.right = '48px';\n      } else {\n        incogBtn.style.right = '';\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Set initial reference and expose globally\n  _syncRailSideFn = _syncRailSideCore;\n  window.syncRailSide = syncRailSide;\n\n  // Restore sidebar side preference\n  if (Storage.get(Storage.KEYS.SIDEBAR_SIDE) === 'right') {\n    document.getElementById('sidebar').classList.add('right-side');\n  }\n  syncRailSide();\n\n  // In-sidebar toggle button \u2014 same behavior as hamburger\n  const sidebarToggleBtn = document.getElementById('sidebar-toggle-btn');\n  if (sidebarToggleBtn) {\n    sidebarToggleBtn.addEventListener('click', (e) => {\n      if (hamburgerBtn) hamburgerBtn.click();\n    });\n  }\n\n  // New chat buttons \u2014 same as clicking brand\n  const chatNewBtn = document.getElementById('chat-new-btn');\n  const sidebarNewChat = document.getElementById('sidebar-new-chat-btn');\n  [chatNewBtn, sidebarNewChat].forEach(btn => {\n    if (btn) btn.addEventListener('click', () => {\n      const brandBtn = document.getElementById('sidebar-brand-btn');\n      if (brandBtn) brandBtn.click();\n    });\n  });\n\n  // Hamburger cycles: full sidebar \u2192 mini \u2192 off \u2192 full\n  // Shift-click swaps sidebar side\n  let _userToggledSidebar = false;\n  let _wasAutoCollapsed = false;\n\n  // Deliberate \"open the sidebar\" used by the mobile swipe gesture (wired at\n  // module scope). It MUST set _userToggledSidebar so the auto-collapse\n  // MutationObserver doesn't immediately re-hide it (the swipe was opening it,\n  // then checkSidebarAutoCollapse re-added .hidden because this flag was unset\n  // \u2014 looked like nothing happened). Mirrors the hamburger's mobile-open path.\n  window._odyOpenSidebar = function(side) {\n    const sidebar = document.getElementById('sidebar');\n    if (!sidebar) return;\n    // On mobile, never open the sidebar while Compare is running \u2014 the panes\n    // own the screen and stray gestures (swipe, dragging a dock chip to the X)\n    // were popping it open. Blocking the open helper covers every path.\n    const cc = document.getElementById('chat-container');\n    if (window.innerWidth < 768 && cc && cc.classList.contains('compare-active')) return;\n    _userToggledSidebar = true;\n    // Optionally place the sidebar on a specific edge (the swipe gesture passes\n    // the direction). Persist it + re-anchor the doc panel, same as a\n    // shift-click on the hamburger.\n    if (side === 'left' || side === 'right') {\n      const wantRight = side === 'right';\n      if (sidebar.classList.contains('right-side') !== wantRight) {\n        sidebar.classList.toggle('right-side', wantRight);\n        try { Storage.set(Storage.KEYS.SIDEBAR_SIDE, side); } catch (_) {}\n        if (documentModule && documentModule.swapSide) { try { documentModule.swapSide(); } catch (_) {} }\n      }\n    }\n    const backdrop = document.getElementById('sidebar-backdrop');\n    if (window.innerWidth < 768 && iconRail) { iconRail.classList.remove('mobile-mini'); iconRail.style.cssText = ''; }\n    sidebar.classList.remove('hidden');\n    if (backdrop && window.innerWidth < 768) backdrop.classList.add('visible');\n    syncRailSide();\n  };\n\n  if (hamburgerBtn) {\n    hamburgerBtn.addEventListener('click', (e) => {\n      e.stopPropagation();\n      const sidebar = document.getElementById('sidebar');\n      if (e.shiftKey) {\n        sidebar.classList.toggle('right-side');\n        Storage.set(Storage.KEYS.SIDEBAR_SIDE, sidebar.classList.contains('right-side') ? 'right' : 'left');\n        syncRailSide();\n        if (documentModule && documentModule.swapSide) documentModule.swapSide();\n        return;\n      }\n\n      _userToggledSidebar = true;\n      const isSidebarVisible = !sidebar.classList.contains('hidden');\n\n      if (window.innerWidth < 768) {\n        // Mobile: full sidebar \u2194 hidden \u2014 simple toggle, no mini rail\n        const backdrop = document.getElementById('sidebar-backdrop');\n        if (iconRail) { iconRail.classList.remove('mobile-mini'); iconRail.style.cssText = ''; }\n\n        if (isSidebarVisible) {\n          // Closing sidebar\n          sidebar.classList.add('hidden');\n          if (backdrop) backdrop.classList.remove('visible');\n        } else {\n          // Mobile: the hamburger always opens the sidebar from the RIGHT.\n          // (Not persisted \u2014 keeps the desktop side preference untouched.)\n          if (!sidebar.classList.contains('right-side')) {\n            sidebar.classList.add('right-side');\n            if (documentModule && documentModule.swapSide) { try { documentModule.swapSide(); } catch (_) {} }\n          }\n          // Opening sidebar \u2014 blur keyboard first, then open after layout settles\n          if (document.activeElement && document.activeElement !== document.body\n              && (document.activeElement.tagName === 'INPUT' || document.activeElement.tagName === 'TEXTAREA')) {\n            document.activeElement.blur();\n            // Wait for keyboard dismiss to settle, then open\n            setTimeout(() => {\n              sidebar.classList.remove('hidden');\n              if (backdrop) backdrop.classList.add('visible');\n              syncRailSide();\n            }, 250);\n          } else {\n            sidebar.classList.remove('hidden');\n            if (backdrop) backdrop.classList.add('visible');\n          }\n        }\n        syncRailSide();\n        return;\n      }\n\n      // Desktop: full sidebar \u2194 mini (icon rail) \u2014 simple toggle\n      if (isSidebarVisible) {\n        sidebar.classList.add('hidden');\n      } else {\n        _wasAutoCollapsed = false;\n        iconRail.classList.remove('rail-hidden');\n        sidebar.classList.remove('hidden');\n      }\n      syncRailSide();\n    });\n  }\n\n  // Icon rail section clicks \u2014 open sidebar and scroll to section\n  if (iconRail) {\n    iconRail.addEventListener('click', (e) => {\n      const btn = e.target.closest('.icon-rail-btn');\n      if (!btn || btn.id === 'rail-new-session' || btn.id === 'rail-delete-session' || btn.id === 'rail-search-btn' || btn.id === 'rail-settings' || btn.id === 'rail-admin') return;\n      const sectionId = btn.dataset.section;\n      if (!sectionId) return;\n      const sidebar = document.getElementById('sidebar');\n      sidebar.classList.remove('hidden');\n      syncRailSide();\n      const section = document.getElementById(sectionId);\n      if (section) {\n        section.scrollIntoView({ behavior: 'smooth', block: 'start' });\n        section.classList.remove('collapsed');\n      }\n    });\n  }\n\n  // Auto-collapse sidebar when window gets small or chat area is squeezed\n  const AUTO_COLLAPSE_WIDTH = 700;\n  const MIN_CHAT_WIDTH = 380; // collapse sidebar if chat gets narrower than this\n\n  function checkSidebarAutoCollapse() {\n    if (_userToggledSidebar) return;\n    const sidebar = document.getElementById('sidebar');\n    if (!sidebar) return;\n    const isHidden = sidebar.classList.contains('hidden');\n\n    // Check if chat area is too narrow (e.g. sidebar + doc panel both open).\n    // BUT \u2014 if a tile-snapped modal exists, IT is what's making chat narrow,\n    // and that's the user's explicit choice. Don't auto-collapse the sidebar\n    // in response, or we get a reactive loop: snap \u2192 narrow chat \u2192 hide\n    // sidebar \u2192 safe-rect changes \u2192 reclamp modal \u2192 new chat width \u2192 ...\n    const chatContainer = document.querySelector('.chat-container');\n    const hasTileSnapped = document.querySelector('.modal-content[data-_tile-zone], .research-pane[data-_tile-zone]');\n    const chatTooNarrow = chatContainer && chatContainer.offsetWidth < MIN_CHAT_WIDTH && !isHidden && !hasTileSnapped;\n", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "9135e93ba10e58cb9f86de98f26f8d7382130dc1", "display_url": "https://github.com/pewdiepie-archdaemon/odysseus/blob/main/static/js/sidebar-layout.js", "display_title": "sidebar-layout.js"}

> {"content": "// Session Management Functions\n// This module handles all session-related operations\n\nimport Storage from './storage.js';\nimport uiModule, { styledPrompt } from './ui.js';\nimport markdownModule from './markdown.js';\nimport chatRenderer from './chatRenderer.js';\nimport { providerLogo } from './providers.js';\nimport { initModelPicker, updateModelPicker } from './modelPicker.js';\nimport themeModule from './theme.js';\nimport spinnerModule from './spinner.js';\n\nconst API_BASE = window.location.origin;\n\nlet sessions = [];\nlet currentSessionId = null;\nlet _sessionNavToken = 0;\nlet _skipAutoSelect = false;\n\nconst SIDEBAR_MAX_VISIBLE = 10;\nconst FOLDER_MAX_VISIBLE = 5;\nlet _showAllSessions = false;\nlet _expandedFolders = {};  // folderName -> true if \"show more\" clicked\nlet _sortMode = Storage.get('odysseus-session-sort') || 'active'; // default to last active\nlet _autoCreateInProgress = false; // guard against recursive auto-create\nconst _INCOGNITO_SESSIONS_KEY = 'ody-incognito-sessions'; // sessionStorage key for incognito session IDs\nconst _isMac = /Mac|iPhone|iPad/.test(navigator.platform);\nconst _mod = _isMac ? '\u2318' : 'Ctrl';\n\nfunction _getIncognitoIds() {\n  try { return JSON.parse(sessionStorage.getItem(_INCOGNITO_SESSIONS_KEY) || '[]'); } catch { return []; }\n}\nfunction _markIncognito(sid) {\n  const ids = _getIncognitoIds();\n  if (!ids.includes(sid)) { ids.push(sid); sessionStorage.setItem(_INCOGNITO_SESSIONS_KEY, JSON.stringify(ids)); }\n}\nfunction _isIncognitoSession(sid) { return _getIncognitoIds().includes(sid); }\nasync function _cleanupIncognitoSessions() {\n  const ids = _getIncognitoIds();\n  if (ids.length === 0) return;\n  // Keep the current active incognito session alive, delete the rest\n  const toDelete = ids.filter(sid => sid !== currentSessionId);\n  if (toDelete.length === 0) return;\n  const keep = ids.filter(sid => sid === currentSessionId);\n  sessionStorage.setItem(_INCOGNITO_SESSIONS_KEY, JSON.stringify(keep));\n  await Promise.all(toDelete.map(sid =>\n    fetch(`${API_BASE}/api/session/${sid}`, { method: 'DELETE' }).catch(() => {})\n  ));\n}\n\n// Research indicator tracking\nconst _researchingSessions = new Set();\nconst _streamingSessions = new Set();   // Background chat streams (not polled against research API)\nconst _completedSessions = new Set();   // Sessions with completed background streams\nlet _researchPollTimer = null;\n\n// Session list keyboard navigation state\nlet _sessionListFocused = false;\n\n/** Clear current session from UI (after delete/archive). */\nfunction _deselectCurrentSession(sid) {\n  if (currentSessionId !== sid) return;\n  currentSessionId = null;\n  uiModule.el('chat-history').innerHTML = '';\n  uiModule.el('current-meta').textContent = 'Odysseus Chat';\n  Storage.remove('lastSessionId');\n  history.replaceState(null, '', window.location.pathname);\n  if (window.chatModule && window.chatModule.showWelcomeScreen) {\n    window.chatModule.showWelcomeScreen();\n  }\n  // Reset send button to idle state\n  const submitBtn = document.querySelector('.send-btn');\n  if (submitBtn) {\n    submitBtn.dataset.mode = '';\n    delete submitBtn.dataset.phase;\n    submitBtn.classList.remove('recording');\n  }\n  if (window._updateSendBtnIcon) window._updateSendBtnIcon();\n}\n\nfunction _removeSessionFromLocalState(sid) {\n  if (!sid) return;\n  const id = String(sid);\n  sessions = sessions.filter(s => String(s.id) !== id);\n  _selectedIds.delete(id);\n  try {\n    const savedOrder = Storage.get('session-order');\n    if (savedOrder) {\n      const orderIds = JSON.parse(savedOrder);\n      if (Array.isArray(orderIds) && orderIds.some(x => String(x) === id)) {\n        Storage.set('session-order', JSON.stringify(orderIds.filter(x => String(x) !== id)));\n      }\n    }\n  } catch (e) {\n    console.warn('Failed to prune deleted session order:', e);\n  }\n  document.querySelectorAll('.list-item[data-session-id]').forEach(el => {\n    if (String(el.dataset.sessionId) === id) el.remove();\n  });\n  _deselectCurrentSession(id);\n}\n\nfunction _normalizeSessionsList(fetched) {\n  if (!Array.isArray(fetched)) return [];\n  const seen = new Set();\n  const unique = [];\n  for (const session of fetched) {\n    if (!session || session.id == null) continue;\n    const id = String(session.id);\n    if (seen.has(id)) continue;\n    seen.add(id);\n    unique.push(session);\n  }\n  return unique;\n}\n\n// Initialize dependencies from app.js (no-op: dependencies now imported directly)\nexport function initDependencies() {}\n\n// \u2500\u2500 Folder state persistence \u2500\u2500\nconst FOLDER_STATE_KEY = 'odysseus-folder-state';\nconst FOLDER_ORDER_KEY = 'odysseus-folder-order';\n\nfunction loadFolderState() {\n  return Storage.getJSON(FOLDER_STATE_KEY, {});\n}\nfunction saveFolderState(state) {\n  Storage.setJSON(FOLDER_STATE_KEY, state);\n}\nfunction loadFolderOrder() {\n  return Storage.getJSON(FOLDER_ORDER_KEY, []);\n}\nfunction saveFolderOrder(order) {\n  Storage.setJSON(FOLDER_ORDER_KEY, order);\n}\n\n/** Get all unique folder names from current sessions. */\nfunction getFolderNames() {\n  const names = new Set();\n  sessions.forEach(s => { if document.createElement('div');\n  noneOpt.className = 'dropdown-item-compact';\n  if (!currentFolder) noneOpt.style.opacity = '0.5';\n  noneOpt.textContent = '(No folder)';\n  noneOpt.addEventListener('click', async (e) => {\n    e.stopPropagation();\n    await moveToFolder(sessionId, '');\n    dropdown.style.display = 'none';\n    sub.style.display = 'none';\n  });\n  sub.appendChild(noneOpt);\n\n  // Existing folders\n  folders.forEach(f => {\n    const opt = document.createElement('div');\n    opt.className = 'dropdown-item-compact';\n    if (f === currentFolder) opt.style.opacity = '0.5';\n    opt.textContent = f;\n    opt.addEventListener('click', async (e) => {\n      e.stopPropagation();\n      await moveToFolder(sessionId, f);\n      // Auto-flip to By serif\",\n};\nconst DEFAULT_FONT = 'mono';\nconst DEFAULT_DENSITY = 'comfortable';\nconst MAX_CUSTOM_THEMES = 8;\n\n// Default background patterns for built-in themes\nconst THEME_DEFAULT_PATTERN = {\n  dark:       'none',\n  light:      'dots',\n  midnight:   'rain',\n  paper:      'dots',\n  cyberpunk:  'synapse',\n  retrowave:  'embers',\n  forest:     'petals',\n  ocean:      'constellations',\n  terminal:   'perlin-flow',\n  organs:     'rain',\n  ume:        'petals',\n  cute:       'sparkles',\n};\n\n// Default effect colors for specific themes (overrides --fg)\nconst THEME_DEFAULT_EFFECT_COLOR = {\n  midnight:   '#ffffff',\n  organs:     '#451616',\n  cute:       '#ff8cb8',\n  ume:        '#f5a0c0',\n};\n\n// Default effect intensity (0..1) per theme. Any theme not listed defaults to 1.\nconst THEME_DEFAULT_INTENSITY = {\n  midnight:   0.5,\n  terminal:   0.8,\n  organs:     0.65,\n};\n\n// Default frosted-glass state per theme. Themes not listed default to false.\nconst THEME_DEFAULT_FROSTED = {\n  lavender:   true,\n};\n\n// \u2500\u2500 Custom theme persistence \u2500\u2500\nfunction _loadCustomThemes() {\n  return Storage.getJSON(CUSTOM_THEMES_KEY, {});\n}\nfunction _saveCustomThemes(obj) {\n  Storage.setJSON(CUSTOM_THEMES_KEY, obj);\n}\nexport function saveCustomTheme(name, colors, opts) {\n  const ct = _loadCustomThemes();\n  // Enforce limit \u2014 allow overwriting existing, block new past max\n  if (!ct[name] && Object.keys(ct).length >= MAX_CUSTOM_THEMES) {\n    return 'limit';\n  }\n  const entry = { ...colors };\n  if (opts) {\n    if (opts.font) entry.font = opts.font;\n    if (opts.density) entry.density = opts.density;\n    if (opts.bgPattern) entry.bgPattern = opts.bgPattern;\n    if (opts.bgEffectColor) entry.bgEffectColor = opts.bgEffectColor;\n    if (opts.bgEffectIntensity !== undefined) entry.bgEffectIntensity = opts.bgEffectIntensity;\n    if (opts.bgEffectSize !== undefined) entry.bgEffectSize = opts.bgEffectSize;\n    if (opts.frosted !== undefined) entry.frosted = !!opts.frosted;\n  }\n  ct[name] = entry;\n  _saveCustomThemes(ct);\n  _syncCustomThemesToServer(ct);\n  initThemeUI();\n  return 'ok';\n}\nexport function deleteCustomTheme(name) {\n  const ct = _loadCustomThemes();\n  delete ct[name];\n  _saveCustomThemes(ct);\n  _syncCustomThemesToServer(ct);\n  initThemeUI();\n}\nfunction _syncCustomThemesToServer(ct) {\n  try {\n    fetch('/api/prefs/custom-themes', {\n      method: 'PUT',\n      headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },\n      credentials: 'same-origin',\n      body: JSON.stringify({ value: ct }),\n    }).catch(e => console.warn('Theme sync (custom) failed:', e));\n  } catch (e) { console.warn('Theme sync (custom) error:', e); }\n}\n\n// --- Syntax color derivation from theme base colors ---\nfunction hexToHSL(hex) {\n  const rgb = hexToRgb(hex) || { r: 0, g: 0, b: 0 };\n  const r = rgb.r / 255;\n  const g = rgb.g / 255;\n  const b = rgb.b / 255;\n  const max = Math.max(r, g, b), min = Math.min(r, g, b);\n  let h, s, l = (max + min) / 2;\n  if (max === min) { h = s = 0; }\n  else {\n    const d = max - min;\n    s = l > 0.5 ? d / (2 - max - min) : d / (max + min);\n    if (max === r) h = ((g - b) / d + (g < b ? 6 : 0)) / 6;\n    else if (max === g) h = ((b - r) / d + 2) / 6;\n    else h = ((r - g) / d + 4) / 6;\n  }\n  return [h * 360, s * 100, l * 100];\n}\n\nfunction hslToHex(h, s, l) {\n  h = ((h % 360) + 360) % 360;\n  s = Math.max(0, Math.min(100, s)) / 100;\n  l = Math.max(0, Math.min(100, l)) / 100;\n  const a = s * Math.min(l, 1 - l);\n  const f = n => { const k = (n + h / 30) % 12; return l - a * Math.max(-1, Math.min(k - 3, 9 - k, 1)); };\n  const toHex = v => Math.round(v * 255).toString(16).padStart(2, '0');\n  return '#' + toHex(f(0)) + toHex(f(8)) + toHex(f(4));\n}\n\nfunction deriveSyntaxColors(colors) {\n  const [fgH, fgS, fgL] = hexToHSL(colors.fg);\n  const [bgH, bgS, bgL] = hexToHSL(colors.bg);\n  const [redH, redS, redL] = hexToHSL(colors.red || '#e06c75');\n  const isDark = bgL < 50;\n  const codeBgL = isDark ? Math.max(bgL - 4, 0) : Math.min(bgL + 4, 100);\n  return {\n    bg: hslToHex(bgH, bgS, codeBgL),\n    fg: colors.fg,\n    keyword: hslToHex((redH + 280) % 360, Math.min(redS + 10, 80), isDark ? 70 : 45),\n    string: hslToHex(40, Math.min(fgS + 20, 70), isDark ? 72 : 42),\n    comment: hslToHex(fgH, Math.max(fgS - 20, 5), isDark ? (fgL * 0.5 + bgL * 0.5) : (fgL * 0.5 + bgL * 0.5)),\n    function: hslToHex(210, Math.min(fgS + 20, 75), isDark ? 70 : 45),\n    // Extra token colors for richer highlighting\n    number: hslToHex(20, Math.min(fgS + 15, 65), isDark ? 68 : 48),\n    builtin: hslToHex(180, Math.min(fgS + 15, 60), isDark ? 65 : 40),\n    variable: hslToHex((fgH + 30) % 360, Math.min(fgS + 5, 60), isDark ? fgL : fgL),\n    params: hslToHex(fgH, Math.max(fgS - 5, 10), isDark ? Math.min(fgL + 8, 85) : Math.max(fgL - 8, 25)),\n  };\n}\n\n// Advanced picker key \u2192 CSS variable mapping\nconst ADV_KEYS = [\n  { key: 'userBubbleBg',       css: '--user-bubble-bg',    label: 'User Chat Bubble', group: 'Chat Bubbles' },\n  { key: 'aiBubbleBg',         css: '--ai-bubble-bg',      label: 'AI Chat Bubble',   group: 'Chat Bubbles' },\n  { key: 'bubbleBorder',       css: '--bubble-border',     label: 'Border Chat Bubble', group: 'Chat Bubbles' },\n  { key: 'sidebarBg',          css: '--sidebar-bg',        label: 'Sidebar Bg',       group: 'Sidebar' },\n  { key: 'brandColor',         css: '--brand-color',       label: 'Odysseus Logo',    group: 'Sidebar' },\n  { key: 'hamburgerColor',     css: '--hamburger-color',   label: 'Hamburger Menu',   group: 'Sidebar' },\n  { key: 'inputBg',            css: '--input-bg',          label: 'Input Bg',         group: 'Chat Input / Prompt Area' },\n  { key: 'inputBorder',        css: '--input-border',      label: 'Input Border',     group: 'Chat Input / Prompt Area' },\n  { key: 'sendBtnBg',          css: '--send-btn-bg',       label: 'Send Btn',         group: 'Chat Input / Prompt Area' },\n  { key: 'sendBtnHover',       css: '--send-btn-hover',    label: 'Send Hover',       group: 'Chat Input / Prompt Area' },\n  { key: 'codeBg',             css: '--code-bg',           label: 'Code Bg',          group: 'Code Blocks' },\n  { key: 'codeFg',             css: '--code-fg',           label: 'Code Text',        group: 'Code Blocks' },\n  { key: 'toggleActive',       css: '--toggle-active',     label: 'Toggle On',        group: 'Controls' },\n];\n\nfunction computeAdvancedDefaults(colors) {\n  const syn = deriveSyntaxColors(colors);\n  const red = colors.red || '#e06c75';\n  return {\n    userBubbleBg: colors.bg,\n    aiBubbleBg: colors.panel,\n    bubbleBorder: colors.border,\n    sidebarBg: colors.panel,\n    brandColor: red,\n    hamburgerColor: colors.fg,\n    inputBg: colors.panel,\n    inputBorder: colors.border,\n    sendBtnBg: red,\n    sendBtnHover: red,\n    codeBg: syn.bg,\n    codeFg: syn.fg,\n    toggleActive: red,\n  };\n}\n\nfunction generateHarmonyColors(accentHex, harmonyType, mode) {\n  const [h, s] = hexToHSL(accentHex);\n  const isDark = mode === 'dark';\n\n  let bgH, bgS, bgL, fgS, fgL, panelL, borderH, borderS, borderL;\n\n  if (harmonyType === 'complementary') {\n    bgH = h; bgS = Math.max(s * 0.15, 3);\n    bgL = isDark ? 13 : 95; fgL = isDark ? 85 : 15; fgS = Math.max(s * 0.2, 5);\n    panelL = isDark ? 8 : 98;\n    borderH = h; borderS = Math.max(s * 0.25, 8); borderL = isDark ? 28 : 75;\n  } else if (harmonyType === 'analogous') {\n    bgH = (h - 30 + 360) % 360; bgS = Math.max(s * 0.12, 3);\n    bgL = isDark ? 14 : 95; fgL = isDark ? 84 : 18; fgS = Math.max(s * 0.15, 5);\n    panelL = isDark ? 9 : 97;\n    borderH = (h + 30) % 360; borderS = Math.max(s * 0.3, 10); borderL = isDark ? 30 : 72;\n  } else if (harmonyType === 'triadic') {\n    bgH = (h + 240) % 360; bgS = Math.max(s * 0.1, 2);\n    bgL = isDark ? 13 : 96; fgL = isDark ? 86 : 14; fgS = Math.max(s * 0.18, 5);\n    panelL = isDark ? 8 : 99;\n    borderH = (h + 120) % 360; borderS = Math.max(s * 0.2, 8); borderL = isDark ? 28 : 74;\n  } else { // monochromatic\n    bgH = h; bgS = Math.max(s * 0.08, 2);\n    bgL = isDark ? 12 : 96; fgL = isDark ? 87 : 13; fgS = Math.max(s * 0.15, 5);\n    panelL = isDark ? 7 : 99;\n    borderH = h; borderS = Math.max(s * 0.2, 6); borderL = isDark ? 26 : 76;\n  }\n\n  return {\n    bg: hslToHex(bgH, bgS, bgL),\n    fg: hslToHex(h, fgS, fgL),\n    panel: hslToHex(bgH, bgS * 0.6, panelL),\n    border: hslToHex(borderH, borderS, borderL),\n    red: accentHex,\n  };\n}\n\nexport function applyColors(colors) {\n  const s = document.documentElement.style;\n  s.setProperty('--bg', colors.bg);\n  s.setProperty('--fg', colors.fg);\n  s.setProperty('--panel', colors.panel);\n  s.setProperty('--border', colors.border);\n > Plan
>
> # Rasputin UI Shell Repair Plan
>
> ## Summary
> Rasputin will not copy Odysseus’ visual feel. We will borrow only its stronger shell mechanics: fast theme boot, no white-screen loading, better sidebar behavior, cleaner session/chat ergonomics, and a more deliberate composer. Rasputin’s own visual identity becomes “professional Warmind”: graphite surfaces, amber-red accent, thin technical lines, restrained local-security language, and no Destiny/Bungie assets.
>
> ## Key Changes
> - Rebuild the Home experience as the primary chat surface:
>   - Remove cards, dashboards, logs, progress blocks, and debug metadata from Home.
>   - Keep a centered empty state, recent conversation thread, markdown assistant output, and collapsed “Details” per response.
>   - Fix encoding/text glitches like `Â·` and random `.` labels everywhere in visible shell text.
>
> - Replace the current crowded sidebar with a focused app shell:
>   - Top-level nav becomes `Home`, `Workspaces`, `Activity`, `Knowledge`, `Models`, `Settings`.
>   - `Agents`, `Sessions`, `Tasks`, `Approvals`, `Audit`, `Memory`, `Skills`, `Telegram`, and `Schedules` move into grouped secondary panels instead of all being first-class nav items.
>   - Add a polished sidebar toggle with persisted states: expanded sidebar, compact icon rail, and mobile drawer.
>   - Sidebar shows recent sessions/tasks only when useful, not as permanent clutter.
>
> - Borrow Odysseus-style shell mechanics, not its aesthetic:
>   - Add early theme boot in `frontend/index.html` so the app does not flash white.
>   - Add a small Rasputin loader overlay until React mounts.
>   - Drive colors through Rasputin CSS variables instead of Bootstrap-heavy visual defaults.
>   - Update browser `theme-color` and favicon/accent state from the active theme.
>   - Self-host or locally bundle any font/asset used by the UI.
>
> - Redesign the composer:
>   - Bottom-pinned, clean, large enough to use comfortably.
>   - Model, workspace, privacy lock, and approval state appear as compact chips above or inside the composer area.
>   - Send button becomes icon-first with accessible label and clear disabled reason.
>   - Enter sends, Shift+Enter newline, stop/cancel state appears during active generation.
>
> - Simplify model/workspace UI on Home:
>   - Main picker shows only the active chat model, readable status, and health.
>   - Hide dry-run, embeddings, tags, raw keys, providers, and endpoint details from the normal path.
>   - Active workspace displays a friendly folder name, never raw `.`.
>   - Workspace switching links to the GUI folder browser instead of requiring manual paths.
>
> - Keep the current React/Vite stack:
>   - Do not revert to Odysseus’ vanilla frontend.
>   - Replace React-Bootstrap shell/card usage in Home/sidebar with custom React components and Rasputin CSS.
>   - Bootstrap may remain for legacy forms during this pass, but the visible shell should no longer look Bootstrap-generated.
>
> ## Public Interfaces
> - No backend API changes required for this pass.
> - Preserve existing endpoints and payloads.
> - Add or preserve test ids:
>   - `home-empty-state`
>   - `chat-composer`
>   - `sidebar-toggle`
>   - `compact-sidebar`
>   - `active-model-chip`
>   - `active-workspace-chip`
>   - `runtime-details-toggle`
>   - `nav-home`
>   - `nav-workspaces`
>   - `nav-activity`
>   - `nav-knowledge`
>   - `nav-models`
>   - `nav-settings`
>
> ## Test Plan
> - Static checks:
>   - `npm.cmd run build`
>   - `node --check` where applicable through Vite/build validation.
>   - `git diff --check`
>
> - UI smoke:
>   - App loads without a white screen.
>   - Sidebar expanded, compact, and mobile drawer states work and persist.
>   - Home shows chat/composer in first viewport on desktop and mobile.
>   - Sending is disabled with a readable model-health reason when no healthy model is selected.
>   - No visible `Â·`, lone `.`, raw workspace `.`, raw model tags, or debug labels appear in Home/sidebar.
>
> - Visual QA:
>   - Capture screenshots for desktop Home, mobile Home, expanded sidebar, compact sidebar, chat with messages, settings entry, and model selector.
>   - Verify professional Warmind theme: graphite/black surfaces, amber-red accents, restrained technical details, no copied Odysseus look.
>
> - Accessibility:
>   - Keyboard can navigate sidebar, composer, model chip, workspace chip, and details panels.
>   - Every icon button has an accessible name.
>   - Focus rings are visible.
>   - Contrast passes for the default Warmind theme.
>
> ## Assumptions
> - Rasputin should borrow Odysseus’ UI mechanics only, not its brand, mood, copy, assets, or full layout.
> - The first implementation pass prioritizes the chat shell over deep settings/tool redesign.
> - The backend/runtime remains unchanged unless UI testing exposes a blocking API bug.
> - No external CDN assets should be introduced because Rasputin is local-first and privacy-focused.
</details>

Got it, that distinction matters. I’ll stop treating Odysseus as a target aesthetic and use it as a parts bin: keep Rasputin’s own Warmind/local-security identity, borrow only the UX mechanics where Odysseus is plainly more mature.

> # Rasputin UI Shell Repair Plan
>
> ## Summary
> Rasputin will not copy Odysseus’ visual feel. We will borrow only its stronger shell mechanics: fast theme boot, no white-screen loading, better sidebar behavior, cleaner session/chat ergonomics, and a more deliberate composer. Rasputin’s own visual identity becomes “professional Warmind”: graphite surfaces, amber-red accent, thin technical lines, restrained local-security language, and no Destiny/Bungie assets.
>
> ## Key Changes
> - Rebuild the Home experience as the primary chat surface:
>   - Remove cards, dashboards, logs, progress blocks, and debug metadata from Home.
>   - Keep a centered empty state, recent conversation thread, markdown assistant output, and collapsed “Details” per response.
>   - Fix encoding/text glitches like `Â·` and random `.` labels everywhere in visible shell text.
>
> - Replace the current crowded sidebar with a focused app shell:
>   - Top-level nav becomes `Home`, `Workspaces`, `Activity`, `Knowledge`, `Models`, `Settings`.
>   - `Agents`, `Sessions`, `Tasks`, `Approvals`, `Audit`, `Memory`, `Skills`, `Telegram`, and `Schedules` move into grouped secondary panels instead of all being first-class nav items.
>   - Add a polished sidebar toggle with persisted states: expanded sidebar, compact icon rail, and mobile drawer.
>   - Sidebar shows recent sessions/tasks only when useful, not as permanent clutter.
>
> - Borrow Odysseus-style shell mechanics, not its aesthetic:
>   - Add early theme boot in `frontend/index.html` so the app does not flash white.
>   - Add a small Rasputin loader overlay until React mounts.
>   - Drive colors through Rasputin CSS variables instead of Bootstrap-heavy visual defaults.
>   - Update browser `theme-color` and favicon/accent state from the active theme.
>   - Self-host or locally bundle any font/asset used by the UI.
>
> - Redesign the composer:
>   - Bottom-pinned, clean, large enough to use comfortably.
>   - Model, workspace, privacy lock, and approval state appear as compact chips above or inside the composer area.
>   - Send button becomes icon-first with accessible label and clear disabled reason.
>   - Enter sends, Shift+Enter newline, stop/cancel state appears during active generation.
>
> - Simplify model/workspace UI on Home:
>   - Main picker shows only the active chat model, readable status, and health.
>   - Hide dry-run, embeddings, tags, raw keys, providers, and endpoint details from the normal path.
>   - Active workspace displays a friendly folder name, never raw `.`.
>   - Workspace switching links to the GUI folder browser instead of requiring manual paths.
>
> - Keep the current React/Vite stack:
>   - Do not revert to Odysseus’ vanilla frontend.
>   - Replace React-Bootstrap shell/card usage in Home/sidebar with custom React components and Rasputin CSS.
>   - Bootstrap may remain for legacy forms during this pass, but the visible shell should no longer look Bootstrap-generated.
>
> ## Public Interfaces
> - No backend API changes required for this pass.
> - Preserve existing endpoints and payloads.
> - Add or preserve test ids:
>   - `home-empty-state`
>   - `chat-composer`
>   - `sidebar-toggle`
>   - `compact-sidebar`
>   - `active-model-chip`
>   - `active-workspace-chip`
>   - `runtime-details-toggle`
>   - `nav-home`
>   - `nav-workspaces`
>   - `nav-activity`
>   - `nav-knowledge`
>   - `nav-models`
>   - `nav-settings`
>
> ## Test Plan
> - Static checks:
>   - `npm.cmd run build`
>   - `node --check` where applicable through Vite/build validation.
>   - `git diff --check`
>
> - UI smoke:
>   - App loads without a white screen.
>   - Sidebar expanded, compact, and mobile drawer states work and persist.
>   - Home shows chat/composer in first viewport on desktop and mobile.
>   - Sending is disabled with a readable model-health reason when no healthy model is selected.
>   - No visible `Â·`, lone `.`, raw workspace `.`, raw model tags, or debug labels appear in Home/sidebar.
>
> - Visual QA:
>   - Capture screenshots for desktop Home, mobile Home, expanded sidebar, compact sidebar, chat with messages, settings entry, and model selector.
>   - Verify professional Warmind theme: graphite/black surfaces, amber-red accents, restrained technical details, no copied Odysseus look.
>
> - Accessibility:
>   - Keyboard can navigate sidebar, composer, model chip, workspace chip, and details panels.
>   - Every icon button has an accessible name.
>   - Focus rings are visible.
>   - Contrast passes for the default Warmind theme.
>
> ## Assumptions
> - Rasputin should borrow Odysseus’ UI mechanics only, not its brand, mood, copy, assets, or full layout.
> - The first implementation pass prioritizes the chat shell over deep settings/tool redesign.
> - The backend/runtime remains unchanged unless UI testing exposes a blocking API bug.
> - No external CDN assets should be introduced because Rasputin is local-first and privacy-focused.

> summarize this chat so i can move to a new one

We built Rasputin into a local-first AI wrapper/workbench concept and progressively hardened its direction.

Current product goal:
Rasputin is a private localhost AI workbench for local model routing, safe file/folder access, RAG, Graphify, task orchestration, and eventually OpenClaw/Hermes-style agents. Core safety rule: local files stay local; models do not get direct internet access; internet access only happens through a brokered MCP/web-search tool with logging, sanitization, and approval gates.

Major architecture decisions:
- Backend: Python/FastAPI-style local server.
- Frontend: moved to React/Vite while keeping the app local-first.
- Runtime: Docker is the reliable launch path.
- Models: vLLM for the main large model, llama.cpp/GGUF for smaller helper models.
- Model manager: registry-driven, with future UI ability to discover, import, test, start/stop local models.
- Safety: approved workspace roots, privacy lock, no direct model internet, approval before risky file/web/Docker actions.
- Storage: local `data/` for memory, indexes, settings, audit, etc., ignored from Git.

Important plans created:
- Production Plan: Rasputin, covering repo hygiene, FastAPI foundation, auth, permissions, workspace registry, RAG v2, Graphify v2, model runtime manager, agent system, coding workbench, product UI, and Docker distribution.
- Stabilization Plan, targeting the model 404 issue, model discovery/repair, structured backend errors, UI reliability, accessibility, and model-health gating.
- Production UI Readiness Plan, targeting a ChatGPT/Gemini/Open WebUI-inspired chat-first UI, simpler model UI, GUI folder selection, persistent preferences, and no debug clutter on Home.
- Agent Runtime Plan, targeting SQLite-backed durable sessions, memory, skills, approvals, Telegram approval integration, sub-agents, context compaction, and typed tool broker.

Recent user correction:
You shared the Odysseus repo as a reference, but clarified you do not want to replicate Odysseus’ feel. You only want to borrow useful UI mechanics from it to fix Rasputin’s weaker parts. Rasputin should keep its own “professional Warmind” identity: graphite/black surfaces, amber-red accents, technical but restrained, no Destiny/Bungie assets.
