> {"content": "// static/js/workspace.js\n//\n// Workspace picker: browse server directories in a draggable modal, choose a\n// folder, and show it as a removable pill in the chat input bar. While set, the\n// chat request sends `workspace` so the agent's file/shell tools are confined\n// to that folder (see routes/chat_routes.py + src/tool_execution.py).\n\nimport Storage, { KEYS } from './storage.js';\nimport uiModule from './ui.js';\nimport { makeWindowDraggable } from './windowDrag.js';\n\nconst API_BASE = window.location.origin;\n// Same folder glyph as the overflow menu item + pill (not an emoji).\nconst _FOLDER_SVG = '<svg class=\"workspace-row-icon\" width=\"15\" height=\"15\" viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\"><path d=\"M3 7a2 2 0 0 1 2-2h4l2 2h8a2 2 0 0 1 2 2v8a2 2 0 0 1-2 2H5a2 2 0 0 1-2-2z\"/></svg>';\nlet _modal = null;\nlet _curPath = '';\n\nexport function getWorkspace() {\n  return Storage.get(KEYS.WORKSPACE, '') || '';\n}\n\nfunction _basename(p) {\n  if (!p) return '';\n  // Handle both POSIX (/) and Windows (\\) separators.\n  const parts = p.replace(/[\\\\/]+$/, '').split(/[\\\\/]/);\n  return parts[parts.length - 1] || p;\n}\n\n// Workspace only applies to agent mode (it scopes the file/shell tools), so the\n// pill + overflow entry are hidden in chat mode, like the bash toggle.\nfunction _isChatMode() {\n  const b = document.getElementById('mode-chat-btn');\n  return !!(b && b.classList.contains('active'));\n}\n\nexport function syncWorkspaceIndicator(path) {\n  const chat = _isChatMode();\n  const pill = document.getElementById('workspace-indicator-btn');\n  const name = document.getElementById('workspace-indicator-name');\n  const overflow = document.getElementById('overflow-workspace-btn');\n  if (pill) {\n    pill.style.display = (path && !chat) ? '' : 'none';\n    pill.classList.toggle('active', !!path);\n    if (path) pill.title = `Workspace: ${path}\\nFile tools are confined here; shell commands start here but are not sandboxed and can reach outside it.\\nClick to clear.`;\n  }\n  if (name) name.textContent = path ? _basename(path) : '';\n  if (overflow) {\n    overflow.style.display = chat ? 'none' : '';\n    overflow.classList.toggle('active', !!path);\n  }\n  // Recompute the \"+\" overflow dot (app.js owns updatePlusDot via this event).\n  try { document.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent('overflow-state-change')); } catch (_) {}\n}\n\n// Called by the agent/chat mode toggle so the pill + overflow entry follow mode.\nexport function applyMode(_mode) {\n  syncWorkspaceIndicator(getWorkspace());\n}\n\nexport function setWorkspace(path) {\n  if (path) Storage.set(KEYS.WORKSPACE, path);\n  else Storage.remove(KEYS.WORKSPACE);\n  syncWorkspaceIndicator(path || '');\n}\n\n/**\n * Validate a manually entered path server-side, then persist the canonical\n * form. Returns {ok, path|null}. Without this, a typo / file path / deleted\n * folder / filesystem root would be stored and shown as active while the\n * backend silently refuses to bind it on every send.\n */\nexport async function vetAndSetWorkspace(path) {\n  try {\n    const res = await fetch(`${API_BASE}/api/workspace/vet?path=${encodeURIComponent(path)}`, { credentials: 'same-origin' });\n    if (!res.ok) return { ok: false, path: null };\n    const data = await res.json();\n    if (data.ok && data.path) {\n      setWorkspace(data.path);\n      return { ok: true, path: data.path };\n    }\n    return { ok: false, path: null };\n  } catch (e) {\n    return { ok: false, path: null };\n  }\n}\n\nexport function clearWorkspace() {\n  setWorkspace('');\n  if (uiModule && uiModule.showToast) uiModule.showToast('Workspace cleared');\n}\n\nasync function _load(path) {\n  const url = `${API_BASE}/api/workspace/browse${path ? `?path=${encodeURIComponent(path)}` : ''}`;\n  const res = await fetch(url, { credentials: 'same-origin' });\n  if (!res.ok) throw new Error(`browse failed: ${res.status}`);\n  return res.json();\n}\n\nfunction _render(data) {\n  _curPath = data.path;\n  const body = _modal.querySelector('#workspace-body');\n  const pathEl = _modal.querySelector('#workspace-cur-path');\n  if (pathEl) {\n    // Reflect the resolved (realpath) location back into the editable field.\n    pathEl.value = data.path;\n    pathEl.title = data.path;\n  }\n  let rows = '';\n  if (data.parent) {\n    rows += `<div class=\"workspace-row workspace-up\" data-path=\"${encodeURIComponent(data.parent)}\">\u2191 ..</div>`;\n  }\n  for (const d of data.dirs) {\n    // Backend supplies the full child path (os.path.join \u2192 cross-platform).\n    rows += `<div class=\"workspace-row\" data-path=\"${encodeURIComponent(d.path)}\">${_FOLDER_SVG}<span>${uiModule.esc(d.name)}</span></div>`;\n  }\n  if (data.truncated) {\n    rows += '<div class=\"workspace-empty\">Too many folders to list. Type or paste a path above to jump in.</div>';\n  }\n  if (!data.dirs.length && !data.parent) rows = '<div class=\"workspace-empty\">No subfolders</div>';\n  body.innerHTML = rows || '<div class=\"workspace-empty\">No subfolders</div>';\n  body.querySelectorAll('.workspace-row').forEach((row) => {\n    row.addEventListener('click', () => _navigate(decodeURIComponent(row.dataset.path)));\n  });\n  // Filesystem roots (and sensitive dirs) can be browsed through but never\n  // bound as the workspace; the backend rejects them too.\n  const useBtn = _modal.querySelector('#workspace-use');\n  if (useBtn) {\n    useBtn.disabled = data.selectable === false;\n    useBtn.title = data.selectable === false ? 'This folder cannot be used as a workspace' : '';\n  }\n}\n\nasync function _navigate(path) {\n  try {\n    _render(await _load(path));\n  } catch (e) {\n    if (uiModule && uiModule.showError) uiModule.showError('Could not open folder');\n  }\n}\n\nfunction _getModal() {\n  if (_modal) return _modal;\n  _modal = document.createElement('div');\n  _modal.id = 'workspace-modal';\n  _modal.className = 'modal';\n  _modal.style.display = 'none';\n  _modal.innerHTML = `\n    <div class=\"modal-content\">\n      <div class=\"modal-header\">\n        <h4><svg width=\"14\" height=\"14\" viewBox=\"0 0 24 24\" fill=\"none\" stroke=\"currentColor\" stroke-width=\"2\" stroke-linecap=\"round\" stroke-linejoin=\"round\" style=\"vertical-align:-2px;margin-right:6px\"><path d=\"M3 7a2 2 0 0 1 2-2h4l2 2h8a2 2 0 0 1 2 2v8a2 2 0 0 1-2 2H5a2 2 0 0 1-2-2z\"/></svg>Select workspace</h4>\n        <button class=\"close-btn\" id=\"workspace-close\" aria-label=\"Close\">\u2716</button>\n      </div>\n      <input type=\"text\" class=\"styled-prompt-input workspace-cur\" id=\"workspace-cur-path\"\n             spellcheck=\"false\" autocomplete=\"off\" autocapitalize=\"off\" autocorrect=\"off\"\n             placeholder=\"Type or paste a folder path, then press Enter\" />\n      <p class=\"muted workspace-note\">File tools are <strong>confined</strong> to this folder. Shell commands start here but are <strong>not sandboxed</strong> and can reach outside it. A workspace scopes the tools; it is not a security boundary.</p>\n      <div class=\"modal-body workspace-body\" id=\"workspace-body\"></div>\n      <div class=\"modal-footer workspace-footer\">\n        <button type=\"button\" class=\"confirm-btn confirm-btn-secondary\" id=\"workspace-cancel\">Cancel</button>\n        <button type=\"button\" class=\"confirm-btn confirm-btn-primary\" id=\"workspace-use\">Use this folder</button>\n      </div>\n    </div>`;\n  document.body.appendChild(_modal);\n  _modal.querySelector('#workspace-close').addEventListener('click', closeWorkspaceBrowser);\n  _modal.querySelector('#workspace-cancel').addEventListener('click', closeWorkspaceBrowser);\n  // Editable path bar: Enter navigates to a typed/pasted folder.\n  _modal.querySelector('#workspace-cur-path').addEventListener('keydown', (e) => {\n    if (e.key === 'Enter') {\n      e.preventDefault();\n      const v = e.target.value.trim();\n      if (v) _navigate(v);\n    }\n  });\n  _modal.querySelector('#workspace-use').addEventListener('click', () => {\n    setWorkspace(_curPath);\n    if (uiModule && uiModule.showToast) uiModule.showToast(`Workspace set: ${_basename(_curPath)}`);\n    closeWorkspaceBrowser();\n  });\n  const content = _modal.querySelector('.modal-content');\n  const header = _modal.querySelector('.modal-header');\n  if (content && header) makeWindowDraggable(_modal, { content, header });\n  return _modal;\n}\n\nexport async function openWorkspaceBrowser() {\n  const modal = _getModal();\n  modal.style.display = 'flex';\n  try {\n    _render(await _load(getWorkspace() || ''));\n  } catch (e) {\n    if (uiModule && uiModule.showError) uiModule.showError('Could not browse folders');\n  }\n}\n\nexport function closeWorkspaceBrowser() {\n  if (_modal) _modal.style.display = 'none';\n}\n\nexport function initWorkspace() {\n  // Restore persisted workspace into the pill on load.\n  syncWorkspaceIndicator(getWorkspace());\n  const overflow = document.getElementById('overflow-workspace-btn');\n  if (overflow) overflow.addEventListener('click', openWorkspaceBrowser);\n  const pill = document.getElementById('workspace-indicator-btn');\n  if (pill) pill.addEventListener('click', clearWorkspace);\n}\n\nexport default { initWorkspace, openWorkspaceBrowser, getWorkspace, setWorkspace, vetAndSetWorkspace, clearWorkspace, syncWorkspaceIndicator, applyMode };", "encoding": "utf-8", "sha": "fd6ab4184f2cb678bd98ea21363f77037ab7a190", "display_url": "https://github.com/pewdiepie-archdaemon/odysseus/blob/dev/static/js/workspace.js", "display_title": "workspace.js"}
> ```

>
> To cover more than the chat screen, I’m pulling the official README demo clips for Research, Compare, Documents, and Notes/Tasks too. That gives us a broader GUI read instead of overfitting to one screenshot.

</details>

I researched Odysseus from its official repo, README demo clips, and frontend source. I did not find a reliable separate video, but the repo includes official animated GUI clips for Chat, Research, Compare, Documents, and Notes/Tasks, plus the actual UI code.

**What Odysseus Does Differently**
Odysseus keeps chat as the center of gravity. Most tools open as draggable/minimizable modal workspaces instead of full page tabs. Its `modalManager.js` supports open/minimize/restore/close, dock chips, remembered dock positions, and modal stacking. Rasputin currently uses route-based tabs plus a task drawer, so every feature becomes a full page. That is why Models/Warsat/Activity can feel heavy.

Odysseus has a stronger model picker workflow. Its composer model picker tracks recent models, favorites, provider grouping, local endpoint probes, offline states, and shortcuts to Cookbook/Settings. Rasputin has the pieces, but they are spread between Home, Models, and Warsat.

Odysseus Cookbook is fit-first. It asks “what fits this machine?” through hardware probing, GPU/RAM toggles, context presets, quantization/runtime filtering, cached scans, running task monitoring, and diagnosis. Rasputin Warsat is safer and more approval-oriented, but still too form-heavy.

Odysseus workspace selection is simpler. It opens from the chat input/overflow, lets the user browse folders, selects one workspace, and uses that to scope agent tools. Rasputin’s Workspaces tab is more powerful because it includes browsing, preview, RAG, Graphify, indexing, and mounts, but that also makes it visually dense.

Odysseus invests heavily in shell mechanics: early theme boot, loader overlay, self-hosted font, route-specific favicon/title/PWA metadata, sidebar auto-collapse, mobile backdrop/swipe behavior, popup dismissal, keyboard shortcuts, and background stream recovery.

**Where Rasputin Is Already Stronger**
Rasputin’s safety model is more explicit. We already separate model access from tool access, approval-gate risky actions, expose Tool Relay, and keep Warsat deployment behind Docker-control checks.

Rasputin’s workspace model is more aligned with your goal: approved folders, Docker mount awareness, read-only defaults, RAG/Graphify integration, and local file companion behavior.

Rasputin’s agent/task visibility is more structured: task details, traces, approvals, tool calls, sub-agent visibility, context governor metadata, and SSE events.

**What We Should Borrow Mechanically**
1. Replace full-page tool overload with “workspace panels.”
   Keep top-level tabs, but let Models, Warsat, Activity, Archive, and Tool Relay open focused side panels/docked panels for detail work.

2. Make Home the command center.
   The chat composer should expose:
   - current model
   - mode
   - workspace
   - attachments/files
   - active run state
   Everything else should be one click away, not always visible.

3. Rebuild Warsat around fit-first flow.
   The first screen should be:
   - hardware detected
   - choose use case
   - recommended models
   - expected VRAM/RAM
   - deploy readiness
   Advanced launch fields should stay hidden until needed.

4. Rework Models into a picker/catalog, not a settings page.
   Normal path:
   - active model
   - health
   - recent/favorite models
   - deployable catalog
   - send to Warsat
   Advanced registry should be hidden.

5. Simplify Workspaces.
   Split into:
   - browse mounted folders
   - active workspace
   - file preview
   - knowledge/indexing drawer
   RAG/Graphify should feel like workspace capabilities, not a separate mental model.

**Sources**
- [Odysseus README](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/pewdiepie-archdaemon/odysseus/dev/README.md)
- [Odysseus app shell](https://github.com/pewdiepie-archdaemon/odysseus/blob/dev/static/index.html)
- [Odysseus main app orchestrator](https://github.com/pewdiepie-archdaemon/odysseus/blob/dev/static/app.js)
- [Odysseus model picker](https://github.com/pewdiepie-archdaemon/odysseus/blob/dev/static/js/modelPicker.js)
- [Odysseus Cookbook](https://github.com/pewdiepie-archdaemon/odysseus/blob/dev/static/js/cookbook.js)
- [Odysseus modal manager](https://github.com/pewdiepie-archdaemon/odysseus/blob/dev/static/js/modalManager.js)

> # Rasputin Odysseus-Mechanics Integration Plan
>
> ## Summary
> Implement a rollback-safe UI mechanics pass on branch `codex/odysseus-mechanics-integration-v1`. The goal is not to copy Odysseus branding, terminology, source code, or visual style. We will borrow the strongest interaction mechanics: chat-first shell, compact model picker, dockable tool panels, fit-first model deployment, simpler workspace picker, and better session ergonomics.
>
> Current repo state matters: there are already uncommitted changes on `codex/gui-mechanics-all-tabs-v1`. First preserve that state with a checkpoint commit and rollback branch before making new edits.
>
> ## Key Changes
> - Repo safety:
>   - Commit current working tree as `Checkpoint GUI mechanics before Odysseus integration`.
>   - Create rollback branch `backup/pre-odysseus-mechanics-integration`.
>   - Create implementation branch `codex/odysseus-mechanics-integration-v1`.
>   - Do not stage `data/`, `testdata/`, `models/`, logs, screenshots, indexes, or secrets.
>
> - Add a Rasputin-native panel/dock system:
>   - Add a reusable `WorkspacePanelHost` for dockable panels with open, close, minimize, restore, Escape close, focus return, and accessible labels.
>   - Use it for Models, Warsat launch details, Activity task details, Workspace knowledge details, Archive, Trials, and Tool Relays.
>   - Keep existing route tabs, but move dense secondary controls into panels so full pages stop feeling overloaded.
>
> - Improve Home shell mechanics:
>   - Keep current Home layout mostly intact.
>   - Replace the current model/mode chip behavior with compact dropdown panels that support recent/favorite models, health state, actual model id, mode routing, and keyboard navigation.
>   - Keep agent lanes, but collapse them into a compact expandable “lanes” control when screen width is tight.
>   - New Chat must always create a real fresh local session and focus the composer.
>
> - Rework Models and Warsat around fit-first flow:
>   - Models page becomes a catalog/picker surface: active model, health, recent/favorites, deployable catalog, and “Prepare in Warsat.”
>   - Warsat becomes a guided “what fits?” workflow: hardware summary, use case, VRAM/RAM fit, quant/runtime filters, recommended models, launch plan, approval, deploy lifecycle.
>   - Advanced Docker/Compose/runtime fields stay behind explicit disclosures.
>   - Keep all deployment approval and Docker-control safeguards unchanged.
>
> - Simplify Workspaces:
>   - Main view shows mounted roots, active workspace, folder/file explorer, and preview.
>   - Move RAG/Graphify indexing/search results into a “Knowledge for this workspace” panel instead of always-visible page clutter.
>   - Keep Docker mount workflow advanced/admin-only.
>   - Keep all file mutation actions out of scope for this pass.
>
> - Improve Activity:
>   - Keep Archive Studio-style Activity layout as the baseline.
>   - Add dockable run detail panels for logs, trace, tools, approvals, and sub-agents.
>   - Reduce always-visible stats and only show high-value live run controls.
>
> ## Public Interfaces
> - No backend API changes required unless a UI flow exposes a missing read-only field.
> - Add frontend-only panel state persisted in localStorage:
>   - open panels
>   - minimized panels
>   - last active panel
>   - recent/favorite model ids
>   - preferred model per mode
> - Preserve existing test IDs where possible and add:
>   - `workspace-panel-host`
>   - `panel-dock`
>   - `panel-minimize`
>   - `panel-restore`
>   - `compact-model-picker`
>   - `compact-mode-picker`
>   - `warsat-fit-flow`
>   - `workspace-knowledge-panel`
>
> ## Test Plan
> - Static and safety:
>   - `git status --short --branch`
>   - `npm.cmd run build`
>   - `git diff --check`
>   - `powershell.exe -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts\check-repo-safety.ps1`
>
> - Automated UI:
>   - `powershell.exe -ExecutionPolicy Bypass -File scripts\test.ps1 -Ui`
>   - Verify Home, Workspaces, Activity, Models, Warsat, Archive, Trials, and Settings still load.
>   - Verify panel open/minimize/restore/close/focus-return behavior.
>   - Verify New Chat creates a fresh session.
>   - Verify model picker shows actual model id and health.
>   - Verify Warsat plan and approval controls remain reachable.
>   - Verify Workspaces explorer and knowledge panel do not overflow.
>
> - Manual GUI QA on `http://127.0.0.1:8989`:
>   - Desktop full width.
>   - Half-monitor split screen.
>   - Mobile width.
>   - Keyboard-only navigation.
>   - Dark and light themes.
>   - No horizontal overflow, hidden close buttons, unlabeled icon buttons, unreadable cards, or excessive nested scrollbars.
>
> ## Assumptions
